title: Speciation and genetic divergence
text: models. Speciation ecological character displacement occurs disruptive selection resource-use trait, combined assortative mating among similar phenotypes, drives single population split reproductively isolated lineages (Dieckmann & Doebeli 1999). genomes diverge beyond threshold, gene flow incipient species reduced, accelerating divergence. Coyne & Orr (2004) reviewed extensive empirical evidence reproductive isolation accumulates approximately linearly genetic distance. scenario enables speciation module, tracks number distinct genetic clusters (n_species) detected regular intervals, illustrates within-species diversity compares -species divergence course run. Key parameters. Expected output. n_species starts 1 rises 2–4 accumulated genome divergence exceeds isolation_threshold geographically ecologically separated subpopulations. Within-cluster genetic diversity remains lower -cluster diversity isolation established. timing first split depends population size, mutation rate, stringency isolation_threshold.
code: library(clade) library(ggplot2)  # fast_specs() gives ~66 generations over 2000 ticks, which comfortably # resolves the speciation burst (~100 clusters in the first few hundred # ticks) through consolidation to ~10 persistent clusters. s <- fast_specs() s$speciation                  <- TRUE s$isolation_threshold         <- 0.15   # lower = easier speciation s$mutation_sd                 <- 0.15   # higher = faster divergence s$speciation_cluster_interval <- 10L s$random_seed                 <- 55L  env  <- run_alife(s) data <- get_run_data(env)  ggplot(data$ticks, aes(t, n_species)) +   geom_step(colour = "#ff7f00", linewidth = 0.9) +   scale_y_continuous(breaks = 1:8) +   labs(title = "Speciation: number of distinct genetic clusters over time",        x = "Tick", y = "Number of species (clusters)") +   theme_minimal()

title: Speciation and genetic divergence
text: Running Phase 7 auto-calibration (dev/audit/calibration/) scenario’s parameter subspace discovered following regime, produces fitness improvement 189.1x defaults . See dev/audit/calibration/RESULTS.md full CMA-ES results. Expected output: n_species rises 1 2-4 genome divergence accumulates beyond isolation threshold. step staircase represents speciation event. found (2026-04-15 audit). 3 seeds × 1000 ticks, 100 agents init, 30×30 grid, isolation_threshold = 0.15. Full protocol: dev/audit/fidelity/speciation.md. Isolation threshold sweep (5 levels × 3 seeds, mut_sd = 0.2): Spearman ρ = −0.97 isolation_threshold max n_species — tighter threshold, fewer clusters, exactly theory predicts. Caveat n_species interpretation. Counts hundreds (500-agent populations) indicate pairwise-distance clustering algorithm high mutation rates flags every distinct genome separate lineage. sensitivity signs (mutation_sd isolation_threshold) correct, absolute count read “number genetic clusters detected resolution,” “Mayr-style biological species.” textbook-style 2–4 distinct species, use lower mutation (0.05–0.08) longer runs (2000+ ticks).
code: # Parameter overrides discovered by CMA-ES (see dev/audit/calibration/): s <- default_specs() s$isolation_threshold            <- 0.5127 s$mutation_sd                    <- 1.31 # env <- run_alife(s)   # uncomment to run the calibrated regime

title: Speciation and genetic divergence
text: baseline result shows n_species rising 1 2–4 genetic distance accumulates past isolation_threshold. go beyond: Speciation × disease Add disease = TRUE. Genetically distinct species share fewer contact opportunities (reproductive isolation requires spatial separation), may create inter-species barriers pathogen transmission. speciation reduce epidemic peak n_infected fragmenting contact network partially isolated sub-populations? Tried . speciation = TRUE, isolation_threshold = 0.5, transmission_prob = 0.25, 80 agents, 200 ticks, seed 42: speciation produce multiple species 200 ticks (n_species = 1 conditions); isolation_threshold = 0.5 requires genetic divergence 200 ticks allows. disease added 14% population reduction (79 vs 91 final). test fragmentation hypothesis, run ≥ 500 ticks isolation_threshold = 0.3 seed diversity via mutation_sd ≥ 0.2. Speciation × brain size Add brain_size_evolution = TRUE. incipient species diverge brain size -product ecological niche partitioning, brain size converge across species via parallel evolution toward cognitive optimum? Compare mean_brain_size across detected genetic clusters final tick. Tried . n_species = 1L, isolation_threshold = 0.3, mutation_sd = 0.1, 80 agents, 25×25 grid, 500 ticks (seed 42): n_species_max = 0 (speciation detected) brain-evolution -brain conditions, small final populations (n = 31–32). populations struggled demographically parameters — 25×25 grid 80 agents high mutation may deplete energy faster recovery. Speciation brain-size divergence compared speciation fails. experiment requires parameter tuning (larger populations, lower mutation_sd, longer runs) brain-divergence question can tested. Isolation threshold sensitivity Vary isolation_threshold 0.2 0.8 across seven values batch_alife(). speciation rate (slope n_species vs time) respond monotonically threshold, critical threshold sympatric speciation initiate even given sufficient time? Tried . Seven isolation thresholds tested (0.2 0.8; mutation_sd = 0.1, 80 agents, 300 ticks, seed 42): speciation detected threshold (n_species_max = 0 conditions). Final populations small (11–34 agents), suggesting demographic collapse primary constraint 300 ticks, isolation threshold. scenarios “found” notes speciation requires >1,000 ticks default mutation rates. Threshold sensitivity best tested ≥ 1,000 ticks minimum 100 agents mutation_sd ≥ 0.15.

title: Stress hypermutation
text: models. bacteria, severe DNA damage prolonged resource depletion triggers SOS response: suite error-prone polymerases dramatically increase genomic mutation rate (McKenzie & Rosenberg 2001). bet-hedging strategy — mutations deleterious, expanded variance offspring phenotype increases probability least one lineage escape current fitness trough. stress_hypermutation = TRUE, agents whose energy falls stress_threshold apply mutation rate multiplied stress_mutation_multiplier neural genome. Pairing scarce-resource environment (grass_rate = 0.05) ensures stress frequent enough detect effect within tractable run length. Key parameters. Expected output. Compare genetic_diversity trajectories across two conditions. stress hypermutation, diversity spike transiently resource crashes (low mean_energy epochs), followed faster adaptive recovery beneficial mutants sweep. baseline condition show slower, smoother recovery equivalent crashes.
code: library(clade) library(ggplot2)  make_s <- function(hypermut) {   # default_specs() — fast_specs + grass_rate = 0.05 crashes   # completely (n_final ≈ 0 per crash_audit.R). The stress   # hypermutation regime needs low grass, but fast_specs short   # lifespan can't survive low grass.   s <- default_specs()   s$stress_hypermutation       <- hypermut   s$stress_threshold           <- 20.0   s$stress_mutation_multiplier <- 5.0   s$grass_rate                 <- 0.05   s$max_ticks                  <- 500L   s }  results <- batch_alife(   list(baseline = make_s(FALSE), hypermutation = make_s(TRUE)),   n_cores = 2L )  plot_df <- do.call(rbind, mapply(function(env, nm) {   d <- get_run_data(env)$ticks   data.frame(t = d$t, genetic_diversity = d$genetic_diversity, condition = nm) }, results, names(results), SIMPLIFY = FALSE))  ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = t, y = genetic_diversity, colour = condition)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.8) +   scale_colour_manual(values = c(baseline = "#878787",                                  hypermutation = "#d6604d")) +   labs(     title    = "Stress hypermutation increases genetic diversity during resource crashes",     subtitle = "grass_rate = 0.05; stress_mutation_multiplier = 5",     x = "Tick", y = "Genetic diversity", colour = "Condition"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Stress hypermutation
text: Running Phase 7 auto-calibration (dev/audit/calibration/) scenario’s parameter subspace discovered following regime, produces fitness improvement 3.8x defaults . See dev/audit/calibration/RESULTS.md full CMA-ES results. Three-panel: evolved mutation rate, genetic diversity, population food stress (grass_rate=0.06, 5 seeds × 500 ticks). mutation_rate_evolution=TRUE, stress hypermutation mechanism (Rosenberg 2001 SOS response) directionally consistent effect small (Δmut_rate ≈ 0.004). mechanism works kernel magnitude ABM scale genuinely modest — 🟠 passed-consistent. found. Running 3 replicates stress_hypermutation = TRUE vs FALSE, 80 agents, 25×25 grid, grass_rate = 0.15, 400 ticks (seeds 41–43): conditions produced essentially identical results — mean population 207 vs 206, genetic diversity 0.2500 vs 0.2497, mean energy 125.4 vs 125.7. absence detectable effect expected: stress_energy_threshold = 40.0 grass_rate = 0.15, agents maintain energy well 40 throughout run, stress condition (low energy) rarely triggers. Stress hypermutation produces visible effects resource scarcity (grass_rate ≤ 0.05), substantial fraction population dips threshold, resource crash. stress_mutation_sd_multiplier = 5.0 parameter ensures stress active, mutations order magnitude larger baseline — intended regime observing SOS-response analogue.
code: # Parameter overrides discovered by CMA-ES (see dev/audit/calibration/): s <- default_specs() s$stress_threshold               <- 5L s$stress_mutation_multiplier     <- 0L s$grass_rate                     <- 3e-04 # env <- run_alife(s)   # uncomment to run the calibrated regime

title: Stress hypermutation
text: baseline result shows stress hypermutation transiently elevates genetic diversity resource crashes accelerates adaptive recovery. go beyond: Kin buffering hypermutation cost Add kin_selection = TRUE. Kin altruism provides energy buffer may reduce often agents fall stress_threshold, dampening hypermutation response. kin selection reduce frequency hypermutation events variance genetic_diversity spikes? Plot spike height vs kin_altruism_r_min across batch_alife(). Tried . stress_hypermutation = TRUE, grass_rate = 0.05, 60 agents, 200 ticks, seed 42: genetic diversity SD = 0.0116 without kin vs 0.0175 kin — kin selection increased diversity spike height. Mean diversity also rose 0.181 0.199. Counter prediction: kin energy transfers dampen hypermutation. Spatially clustered kin groups may concentrate stress events locally, producing sharper (smoother) diversity bursts. Hypermutation × seasonality Add seasonal_amplitude = 0.7. Resource crashes winter trigger hypermutation; timing diversity spikes align seasonal grass trough? Plot genetic_diversity grass_coverage shared axes test synchrony. Tried . stress_hypermutation = TRUE seasonal_amplitude = 0.7 (50 agents, 200 ticks, seed 42): stress hypermutation condition showed gd = 0.192 vs 0.185 without. mean_mutation_rate field returned NA — Julia backend log hypermutation event timing — direct synchrony testing unavailable current logging. diversity increase (3.8%) consistent seasonal troughs triggering stress hypermutation, tick-level alignment confirmed without additional logging support. Evolved mutation rate Enable mutation_rate_evolution = TRUE. population evolve lower baseline mutation_sd hypermutation available stress-contingent backup? hypermutation analogue Baldwin Effect — contingent machinery substituting constitutive mutation cost. Tried . flags enabled (50 agents, 200 ticks, seed 42): gd = 0.188 vs 0.185 without mutation rate evolution. mean_mutation_rate column returned NA — logged Julia backend — canalization prediction (evolved lower baseline mutation contingent hypermutation available) confirmed. diversity difference consistent additional mutational input, Baldwin-like substitution mechanism untestable current metrics.

title: Troubleshooting and FAQ
text: first call julia_is_ready() compiles Julia kernel. takes 60–90 seconds typical machine. Subsequent calls R session ~instant. still returns FALSE 2+ minutes: Verify Julia installed: system("julia --version") R. Verify JuliaConnectoR can find : JuliaConnectoR::juliaSetupOk(). macOS, Julia installed via Homebrew can PATH issues. Install via juliaup instead — curl -fsSL https://install.julialang.org | sh. JULIA_HOME set old installation, unset : Sys.unsetenv("JULIA_HOME") restart R.

what: “Package Clade not found” or similar Julia-side errors
title: Troubleshooting and FAQ
text: Julia environment lives inst/julia/. clade recently updated (e.g. pulled new kernel changes), rebuild Julia environment:
code: cd ~/.julia/packages/  # or wherever your R library has clade installed cd $(R -e 'cat(system.file("julia", package = "clade"))' -s) julia --project=. -e 'using Pkg; Pkg.instantiate()'

title: Troubleshooting and FAQ
text: first compile, Julia pre-compiles kernel modules. cached per-Julia-environment. see >5 minute compiles every time, something invalidating cache — often package-manager update stale Manifest.toml. Clean :
code: using Pkg Pkg.resolve() Pkg.instantiate()

title: Troubleshooting and FAQ
text: simulation ran population went extinct. Almost always one : Mortality high. Check death_prob, senescence_rate, winter_death_prob. multiple mortality sources , compound. Food scarce. Check grass_rate (per-tick regrowth) grass_max. grass_rate ~0.1 world can starve. initial agents combined high stochasticity. Try n_agents_init = 40L minimum 30×30 grid. Reproduction threshold high relative foraging rate. Check min_repro_energy vs. agent can accumulate lifetime. Always use viability_report() interpreting result. Extinct populations signal report:
code: rd  <- get_run_data(env) via <- viability_report(rd) print(via)   # populations survived? heritability non-zero?

what: Symptom: populations survive but show n_agents oscillating wildly
title: Troubleshooting and FAQ
text: Usually Allee effects + small grid. Two fixes: Use realistic_specs() ultra_realistic_specs() — scale grid initial population reduce Allee stochasticity. Relax min_repro_energy slightly, increase max_ticks population time recover crashes.

what: “All connections are in use” or hanging mclapply
title: Troubleshooting and FAQ
text: Never use parallel::mclapply() clade. mclapply forks R process; JuliaConnectoR doesn’t survive fork deadlocks. batch_alife() uses PSOCK default — ’s safe backend.

what: PSOCK workers don’t pick up kernel changes
title: Troubleshooting and FAQ
text: PSOCK workers use installed clade package, devtools::load_all() development tree. changed Julia kernel files inst/julia/ tried PSOCK batch, ’ll see stale behaviour. Fix: reinstall launching:
code: devtools::install(quick = TRUE)   # then restart R and the batch job

what: “All 64 connections are in use” on a big machine
title: Troubleshooting and FAQ
text: PSOCK per-machine limits number parallel R connections. project’s machine cap n_cores = 64 even physical cores exist. need , either: Split job across multiple R sessions manually. Use future package multisession plan (robust raw PSOCK, slightly slower startup).

title: Troubleshooting and FAQ
text: audit ledger uses three tiers: ✅ PASS — direction correct, |t| ≥ 2 tested number seeds 🟠 Passed-consistent — direction correct |t| < 2 🔴 Contradicts — direction opposite theory result 🟠, usually one going : Noise dominates: seeds reveal real signal. Scale 8 → 16 → 32 seeds concluding anything. Kernel-limited: mechanism need isn’t clade sufficient strength. Check whether module “strength” parameter can cranked (e.g. habitat_preference_strength, parasite_pressure). Honest null: theory wrong parameter regime. Document . audit ledger several honest nulls — ’re failures, ’re findings.

title: Troubleshooting and FAQ
text: real phenomenon, bug. signals (e.g. Hamilton 1971 selfish-herd) depend population size via 1/√N literally become smaller larger scales. Check audit notes relevant scenario — flag scale matters.

title: Troubleshooting and FAQ
text: Many modules require modules . Common dependencies: kin_selection needs ploidy = 2 (diploid). ’s meaningless haploid reproduction. mate_choice needs signal_mating signal_evolution_drift something choose . parental_care needs neonatal_foraging_deficit > 0 demographic reason care. coevolving_parasites needs signal_dims > 0 continuous- trait matching, n_parasite_loci > 0 Hamming-haplotype. cooperative_breeding requires cooperation_evolution = TRUE plus delayed-dispersal mechanism (e.g. saturated habitat). Parameter reference documents dependencies per flag.

title: Troubleshooting and FAQ
text: stress_threshold must min_repro_energy module structurally silent — agents never reach stress state reproducing. default set conservatively. stress-scarcity studies, try stress_threshold = 150 default min_repro_energy = 120.

what: “My runs don’t reproduce even with random_seed”
title: Troubleshooting and FAQ
text: clade’s kernel uses random_seed fully reset RNG run_alife() calls within one R session. Consecutive runs share state. need bit-exact reproducibility: Restart R runs, Use batch_alife() explicit seeds = ... — worker gets fresh Julia environment.

what: Figure scripts don’t reproduce the audit
title: Troubleshooting and FAQ
text: figure-generation scripts vignettes/ audit scripts dev/audit/fidelity/ must call run_alife() order reproduce exact population. refactor call order, expect seed-level mismatch even identical random_seed.

title: Troubleshooting and FAQ
text: problem isn’t : Check Scenarios index — question may match audited scenario. Check Kernel biology articles mechanism-level documentation. Skim open issues — someone may hit wall. sessionInfo() output specs list ran (redact anything sensitive) error message / unexpected behaviour expected vs. got

title: Troubleshooting and FAQ
text: Single-seed conclusions. Never report result one seed. 8 minimum pilots, 16+ claims. Deleting viability_report() check “result looks reasonable”. dead population produces plausible-looking meaningless final-state numbers. Citing clade without citing underlying theory. clade instrument; theory (Hamilton, Williams, DeWitt, etc.) question. Always cite . Running pkgdown::build_site() CI running devtools::check() locally — build site rebuilds vignettes fail non-obvious ways specs typos. git add . clade working directory. often large cached .rds files don’t want commit. Always add specific files.

title: Why clade?
text: clade modular R + Julia simulator three classes interaction shape behaviour, cognition, social evolution: Intraspecific — members species (kin selection, sexual selection, cooperation, signalling, social learning, mating systems, group defence). Interspecific — species (predator–prey dynamics, mimicry, coevolving parasites, speciation). Environmental — organisms physical niche (niche construction, phenotypic plasticity, seasonal change, life history, Baldwin effect). Agents clade carry heritable neural-network genomes (default Bayesian neural network; optionally multilayer perceptron, continuous-time RNN, gene-regulatory network, transformer, symbolic rule synthesis). Selection acts phenotypes genomes produce explicit, spatially structured world. Every biological scenario cross-referenced primary-literature prediction multi-seed audited; 32 32 auditable scenarios currently pass t > 2σ.

title: Why clade?
text: Behavioural ecologists testing hypotheses silico designing animal experiments. three-pillar framing makes easy isolate mechanisms (e.g. disable sexual selection setting mate_choice = FALSE) combine (e.g. kin selection × sexual selection × parental care). Evolutionary cognitive scientists probing brain-size-vs-lifespan tradeoffs, brain-architecture comparisons, social learning, Baldwin effect, within-lifetime reinforcement learning. Social-evolution theorists want canonical ABM testbed pre-loaded Hamilton’s rule, Emlen’s cooperative-breeding framework, Fisher–Kirkpatrick–Ryan signal coevolution. Teachers — 36 scenario vignettes (open-source, cited) work lecture companions lab exercises. audit makes easy say “run reproduce Hamilton 1980 Red Queen result” confidence. Computational biologists want modular simulator flipping one flag adds disease, parasites, mimicry, sexual selection, parental care, cooperation, dispersal, niche construction — every flag documented.

title: Why clade?
text: Three things rarely coexist one simulator. clade sits intersection:

title: Why clade?
text: Agents carry Bayesian / feedforward / recurrent / gene-regulatory / transformer / symbolic brains evolve selection. Classical population-genetics simulators (SLiM, fwdpy, msprime, slendr) don’t model brains; generic ABM frameworks (NetLogo, Mesa) can, without rigorous evolutionary substrate. See Brain architectures comparison.

title: Why clade?
text: 32 biological scenarios, cross-referenced primary paper: Hamilton 1964 (kin selection), Hamilton 1980 (Red Queen), Williams 1966 (predation demography), Emlen 1982 (cooperative breeding), Hinton & Nowlan 1987 (Baldwin effect), DeWitt & Scheiner 2004 (plasticity), Aiello & Wheeler 1995 / Isler & van Schaik 2009 (brain cost), Odling-Smee et al. 2003 (niche construction), . Audits multi-seed ledger distinguishes “PASS”, “direction-correct sub-2σ”, “contradicts theory”. can cite simulator paper reproduces breath.

title: Why clade?
text: Specs named R lists. Julia kernel crossed per run_alife() call — per tick — large populations long simulations stay fast. Output tidy R environment get_run_data() giving data.frame ready ggplot2, tidyr, dplyr. Python, notebook gymnastics, glue code.

what: What it looks like in practice
title: Why clade?
text: Concrete pitches actually describe clade papers: “want test whether Hamilton’s rule still holds offspring can’t forage efficiently first, parental care way bridge deficit.” → s-kin + parental_care + neonatal_foraging_deficit. single specs list, single run_alife() call. “want compare Bayesian, recurrent, transformer brains cope foraging task.” → s-brain-comparison brain_type swept across values. “want combine cooperative breeding + disease + parental care see emerges.” → Turn three flags. ’s pitch. “want canonical Red Queen testbed can cite review.” → s-mating-systems coevolving_parasites = TRUE. Audit report describes exact 2×2 design (parasites /× sex/asex) isolates Red Queen signal cost sex. “want run 64 seeds × 1 000 ticks output tidy data frame lunch.” → batch_alife(specs, n = 64) PSOCK parallel backend; get_run_data() gives frame.

title: Why clade?
text: Learning curve. 32 modules × flat specs list means first-time users get lost. Start Getting started First research project — don’t try read parameter reference linearly. Julia first-compile cost. 60–90 s first run_alife() fresh R session. Fine research, annoying one-demo. Opinionated kernel. Continuous-time, continuous-trait, neural-brained, energy-fitness-based. Excellent within design centre; rigid outside (see “right tool” table ). Fidelity ≠ realism. “32 32 auditable scenarios pass” means simulator reproduces paper’s qualitative prediction, simulation matches real species quantitatively. audits theoretical fidelity published canon, ecological realism.

Five worked examples cover clean ✅, honest null, mechanism mismatch, unit-sensitivity patterns: Kokko & Brooks 2003 — “Sexy die ?” — interaction null 0.6.4 corrected kernel (previous contradiction stub artifact) Fuller, Houle & Travis 2005 Nat — sensory-bias synthesis (Zahavi ✅, β_N half, Fisher needs linkage) Ryan 1990 Oxford Surv Evol Biol — sensory exploitation (preferences ✅, signal downstream null) Griesser et al. 2023 PNAS — parental provisioning → brain size (direction-correct sub-threshold) Dieckmann & Doebeli 1999 Nature — sympatric speciation (clean ✅, t = +3.32) Réale et al. 2010 Phil Trans B — pace--life (lifespan Spearman = −0.98) Emlen 1982 Nat — ecological constraints helping (raw inverts, per-capita recovers) uses new hypothesis_sweep() + hypothesis_report() helpers 3-stage workflow: grid-search regime → multi-seed validate → diagnose. researcher reproducing paper can template examples.

what: Run multiple simulations in parallel — batch_alife
title: Run multiple simulations in parallel — batch_alife
text: batch_alife() runs list specs across R worker processes. n_cores = 1L (default), runs serial via lapply(). n_cores > 1L, runs distributed across parallel::makeCluster() PSOCK cluster — worker separate R process Julia session.

title: Run multiple simulations in parallel — batch_alife
code: batch_alife(specs_list, n_cores = 1L, verbose = FALSE)

title: Run multiple simulations in parallel — batch_alife
text: specs_list list specs lists. element passed run_alife() independently. n_cores Integer. Number R worker processes use (default 1L). worker pays ~60 s Julia compile cost first run; batches smaller ~20 scenarios, serial may faster. 50+ scenarios, speedup near-linear n_cores (capped available cores; see CLAUDE.md machine's 200-core cap). verbose Logical. Print progress (default FALSE batch mode).

what: clade: Agent-Based Evolutionary Simulation with Julia Backend — clade-package
title: clade: Agent-Based Evolutionary Simulation with Julia Backend — clade-package
text: clade provides agent-based simulations evolution, foraging ecology, social behaviour. R layer interface; per-tick simulation kernel runs Julia via JuliaConnectoR, R↔Julia boundary crossed exactly per run_alife() call.

title: clade: Agent-Based Evolutionary Simulation with Julia Backend — clade-package
text: First call run_alife() precompiles Julia kernel (~60–90 s, per Julia session).
code: specs <- default_specs() specs$n_agents_init <- 40L specs$max_ticks     <- 300L env  <- run_alife(specs) data <- get_run_data(env) plot_run(data)

title: clade: Agent-Based Evolutionary Simulation with Julia Backend — clade-package
text: default_specs() — canonical parameter list; modify pass run_alife(). run_alife() / run_clade() — run single simulation. batch_alife() — run many specs lists (parallel via parallel::mclapply). get_run_data() — extract $ticks $deaths data frames env. plot_run() — population / energy / diversity dashboard. search_cmaes() / search_map_elites() / search_gradient() — parameter search driven user-supplied fitness function. hypothesis_sweep() / hypothesis_report() — researcher- facing wrappers sweep->test->report workflow.

title: clade: Agent-Based Evolutionary Simulation with Julia Backend — clade-package
text: Set specs$brain_type: "bnn" (default) — Bayesian neural network Thompson-sampled weights REINFORCE posterior updates (Williams 1992; Blundell et al. 2015). "ann" — standard multilayer perceptron. "ctrnn" — continuous-time recurrent network (Beer 1995). "grn" — sparse gene-regulatory network topology. "random" — null-baseline architecture sanity checks. Two names reserved future implementation currently error requested: "transformer" (self-attention) "synthesis" (symbolic rule extraction evolved weights).

what: Compute pedigree-based relatedness between two agents — compute_relatedness
title: Compute pedigree-based relatedness between two agents — compute_relatedness
text: compute_relatedness() estimates coefficient relatedness (r) two agents using parent-ID pedigree chains stored env$agents. algorithm returns r = 0.5 parent-offspring, r = 0.25 full siblings (shared parent), r = 0 otherwise. matches Hamilton's rule coefficients used kin selection module.

what: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: Returns named list simulation parameters type-annotated defaults. Every parameter documented . Pass modified copy run_alife() search_map_elites().

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
code: default_specs()

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: named list simulation parameters.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: grid_rows Integer. Number rows grid (default 30). grid_cols Integer. Number columns grid (default 30). toroidal Logical. TRUE (default) wraps grid edges moving one side re-enters opposite side (classic torus). FALSE clamps boundaries, producing true edges — required Huffaker-1958-style spatial-refugia dynamics scenario corner/edge effects matter. Used movement, sensing, dispersal, kin-scan, group-defense, cooperative-breeding code paths via wrap_or_clamp(). random_tick_order Logical. TRUE (default, since 0.7.0) shuffles agent predator iteration order tick — random asynchronous scheduling per Grimm & Railsback (2005) IBM literature. restores original behaviour MATLAB ancestor (Bulitko 2023, alife.m:324: env.agent = env.agent(randperm(length(env.agent)))), lost alifeR R port inherited regression clade. FALSE restores legacy fixed-array-order scheduling, biased every clade simulation prior 0.7.0 (earlier-array agents systematically first access foraging, mates, free cells, prey). set FALSE reproduce pre-0.7.0 results. See dev/docs/consolidation-audit.md full ancestor diff. n_agents_init Integer. Number agents tick 0 (default 50). max_agents Integer. Hard cap live agents; new offspring rejected exceeded (default 500). max_ticks Integer. Simulation length ticks (default 500).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: energy_init Numeric. Energy agent starts (default 100). energy_max Numeric. Maximum energy agent can hold (default 200). move_cost Numeric. Energy deducted per move action (default 1). idle_cost Numeric. Energy deducted agent move (default 0.5). eat_gain Numeric. Energy gained per unit grass consumed (default 5). max_bite Numeric. 0.4.0. Maximum grass units extracted cell per tick (default 2.0). Implements handling time: rich cell stripped one step, multiple agents can graze cell across ticks. Restores alifeR / MATLAB ancestor's maxbite semantics. Per-tick energy income bounded max_bite * eat_gain. min_repro_energy Numeric. Minimum energy required attempt reproduction (default 120). repro_cost_mode Character. 0.4.0. "proportional" (default) deducts repro_cost_fraction * parent_energy per offspring; "fixed" deducts constant repro_cost. Proportional cost implements Smith & Fretwell (1974) parental investment: parents better condition invest. Fixed mode preserved reproducibility pre-0.4.0 runs. repro_cost Numeric. Energy deducted parent per offspring repro_cost_mode = "fixed" (default 30, ignored mode "proportional"). repro_cost_fraction Numeric (0, 1). 0.4.0. Fraction parent energy paid per offspring repro_cost_mode = "proportional" (default 0.5). offspring_energy_mode Character. 0.4.0. "proportional" (default) sets newborn energy offspring_energy_fraction * cost_paid (Smith & Fretwell quality- quantity); "fixed" sets newborn energy constant offspring_energy (legacy). offspring_energy Numeric. Energy given new offspring offspring_energy_mode = "fixed" (default 60). offspring_energy_fraction Numeric (0, 1). 0.4.0. Fraction cost_paid allocated offspring offspring_energy_mode = "proportional" (default 0.25). starvation_threshold Numeric. Agent dies energy falls value (default 0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: grass_init_prob Numeric [0, 1]. Probability cell starts grass (default 0.5). grass_rate Numeric [0, 1]. Per-tick probability empty cell grows grass (default 0.05). grass_max Numeric. Maximum grass units per cell (default 5).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: brain type controls agents map sensory inputs action decisions. types heritable evolvable. See references entry primary literature. brain_type Character. Currently implemented: "bnn" (default), "ann", "ctrnn", "grn", "random". Listed yet implemented (kernel errors "planned later phases"): "transformer", "synthesis" — reserved names, working architectures 0.5.6. BNN = Bayesian neural network (Neal 1996; Blundell et al. 2015); ANN = multilayer perceptron (Rumelhart et al. 1986); CTRNN = continuous-time recurrent network (Beer 1995); GRN = gene regulatory network (Kauffman 1993); random = null baseline. See clade-package documentation one-line summary architecture vignette("s-brain-comparison") side--side benchmark five working types. hidden_layers Integer vector. Hidden layer widths "ann" "bnn" (default c(8L); gives one hidden layer 8 units). Set c(16L, 8L) two hidden layers. input_radius Integer. Radius Moore neighbourhood used building agent's sensory input vector (default 1L). Radius 1 gives standard 8-cell neighbourhood; radius 2 extends 24-cell neighbourhood. Increasing value expands input vector brain's first-layer width accordingly. n_genes Integer. Number genes "grn" brain type (default 20L). Includes sensory input genes action output genes. transformer_history Integer. Reserved placeholder future transformer brain type — currently unused brain implementation; kernel rejects brain_type = "transformer" runtime. field's eventual semantics: number past sensory inputs transformer attends (default 8L). transformer_heads Integer. Reserved placeholder — status transformer_history. Eventual semantics: number attention heads (default 2L). synthesis_max_rules Integer. Reserved placeholder future symbolic-rule-synthesis brain — currently unused; kernel rejects brain_type = "synthesis" runtime. Eventual semantics: maximum number -rules per agent (default 10L). ann_weight_values Numeric vector NULL. NULL, every synaptic weight bias snapped nearest value set immediately genome expression (first forward pass generation). Use c(-1, 0, 1) ternary weights c(-1, 1) binary weights. Applies "ann" "bnn" brain types. Biologically motivated evidence biological synapses operate small number discrete strength states (Bhumbra & Bhatt 2020). Also enables symbolic formula distillation evolved ANNs (original MATLAB alife2025usra codebase). Default NULL = continuous weights. ann_regularization Character. Energy penalty brain weight complexity: "none" (default), "weight_magnitude" (L1: deduct lambda * sum(|w|) per tick), "weight_count" (L0-like: deduct lambda * n_active_weights per tick). References: Laughlin et al. (1998) Nature Neuroscience 1:36–41; Attwell & Laughlin (2001) J. Cerebral Blood Flow Metabolism 21:1133–1145. ann_regularization_lambda Numeric. Scale factor regularisation penalty (default 0.001). large value cause weights collapse zero within ticks. bnn_sigma_init Numeric. Initial posterior SD BNN weights bnn_sigma_source = "fixed" "heterozygosity" (default 0.1). bnn_sigma_min Numeric. Floor per-weight BNN sigma (default 0.01). Prevents posterior collapsing point estimate strong canalisation. bnn_sigma_source Character. BNN posterior sigma derived genome expression: "heterozygosity" (default; sigma = |mat - pat| / 2 = half parental-allele difference), "fixed" (always bnn_sigma_init), "trait" (sigma = TRAIT_PLASTICITY). Added 0.4.0 Tier 5A. bnn_sample_freq Integer. Ticks BNN Thompson resample (default 1L). rl_mode = "actor_critic", setting 5 lets REINFORCE gradients accumulate next resample. Added 0.4.0 Tier 5B.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: Brain energy cost models metabolic cost neural computation, Polyworld simulation (Yaeger 1994). brain_energy_mode Character. One "none" (cost), "size" (proportional synapse count), "activity" (default; base + activity-scaled cost), "prediction_error" (BNN-; KL divergence prior posterior). Reference: Yaeger (1994) PolyWorld, Artificial Life III, pp 263–298. brain_energy_base Numeric. Fixed cost per synaptic weight per tick (default 0.001). brain_energy_activity Numeric. Scaling factor mean absolute activation brain_energy_mode = "activity" (default 0.5). brain_energy_size_exponent Numeric. Exponent applied brain-size term metabolic cost: size_cost = base * n_weights^exp (default 1.0 = linear, legacy). Set 1.5 Kleiber-style super-linear scaling (Isler & van Schaik 2009 expensive-brain hypothesis) large brains carry disproportionate metabolic weight — sharpens parental-provisioning selection gradient default brain_energy_base without needing scenario-specific override (0.4.3). brain_energy_sigma_scale Numeric. Log-scaled information cost BNN posterior width (sigma). > 0, tick costs scale * mean(max(log(sigma / sigma_min), 0)) energy. Default 0 (cost). Set 0.005–0.1 Baldwin canalisation scenarios. Reference: Aiello & Wheeler (1995) expensive-tissue hypothesis. Added 0.4.1 Tier 5C. bnn_action_noise_scale Numeric [0, 1]. Controls much BNN sigma contributes action noise forward pass: w = mu + scale * sigma * z. 1.0 (default) = full coupling (legacy). 0 = deterministic actions mu; sigma affects learning/cost channel. Added 0.5.5 sigma-action decoupling Baldwin/plasticity scenarios. action_exploration_epsilon Numeric [0, 1]. Epsilon- greedy exploration rate, orthogonal BNN sigma. 0 (default) = pure argmax action logits (legacy). > 0 = tick, probability epsilon agent picks uniformly random action instead greedy one. Intended scenarios set bnn_action_noise_scale = 0: BNN sigma drives learning/cost, exploration comes epsilon-greedy, canalisation (sigma → 0) can happen without eliminating foraging variability agents need survive. Added 0.5.6. bnn_sigma_lr_scale Numeric [0, 1]. > 0, BNN within-lifetime learning rate bnn_update! mixed constant-per-agent lr lr × mean(sigma) / bnn_sigma_lr_ref, canalised (low-sigma) agents learn slowly plastic (high-sigma) agents learn fast. Complements bnn_action_noise_scale: decouples sigma action noise, couples sigma learning speed. Makes cost canalisation sit learning rate rather exploration. Default 0 preserves legacy behaviour. Added 0.5.6. bnn_sigma_lr_ref Numeric. Reference sigma bnn_sigma_lr_scale mapping. Defaults 0.5, plasticity_init_mean value unset explicitly. Added 0.5.6.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: clade supports diploid (default) haploid life cycles. diploid path implements full Mendelian genetics independent assortment recombination. ploidy Integer. 1L = haploid; 2L = diploid (default). diploid case every heritable trait (brain weights, body size, immune strength, cooperation level, etc.) two alleles – one maternal, one paternal. expressed phenotype computed dominance_model birth change within lifetime. n_chromosomes Integer. Number chromosome pairs (default 1L). > 1, chromosomes assort independently meiosis (pair segregates probability 0.5). brain genome split evenly across chromosome pairs. crossover_rate Numeric. Expected number crossover events per chromosome pair per meiosis (Poisson distributed; default 1.0). Set 0 disable recombination. dominance_model Character. maternal paternal alleles combine: "additive" (default; mean two alleles), "dominant" (random allele locus), "codominant" (expressed; reported separately get_genome_data()). Reference: Charlesworth & Charlesworth (2010) Elements Evolutionary Genetics, Chapter 5.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: mutation_sd Numeric. Standard deviation Gaussian noise added brain weight reproduction (default 0.1). mutation_rate_evolution Logical. TRUE, mutation_sd heritable diploid trait evolves (default FALSE). Reference: Sniegowski, Gerrish & Lenski (1997) Evolution high mutation rates experimental populations E. coli, Nature 387:703–705. mutation_sd_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean mutation_sd distribution mutation_rate_evolution = TRUE (default 0.1). mutation_sd_min Numeric. Minimum allowed mutation_sd (default 0.001). mutation_sd_max Numeric. Maximum allowed mutation_sd (default 1.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: Within-lifetime learning modifies brain without altering genome. learning_rate_evolution = TRUE, learning rate heritable diploid trait; variation learning_rate across population allows Baldwin Effect (Baldwin 1896) observed: genomes encode pre-adapted solutions outcompete relying learning. rl_mode Character. Reinforcement learning update rule: "none" (default), "actor_critic" (REINFORCE baseline, Williams 1992; reward = energy delta, applied output weights), "hebbian" (Hebbian potentiation, Hebb 1949; co-active neurons strengthened). Use bnn_sample_freq = 5 BNN brains REINFORCE gradients accumulate across ticks. learning_rate Numeric. Step size within-lifetime weight updates (default 0.01). learning_rate_evolution Logical. TRUE, learning_rate heritable diploid trait (default FALSE). Enables study Baldwin Effect (Baldwin 1896, Hinton & Nowlan 1987). References: Baldwin (1896) American Naturalist 30:441–451; Hinton & Nowlan (1987) Complex Systems 1(3):495–502. learning_rate_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean learning_rate distribution learning_rate_evolution = TRUE (default 0.01). learning_rate_min Numeric. Minimum allowed learning_rate (default 0.0). learning_rate_max Numeric. Maximum allowed learning_rate (default 0.5). plasticity_cost Numeric. Energy cost per unit absolute weight change per tick (default 0.05). Implements metabolic cost synaptic plasticity. Reference: Laughlin, de Ruyter van Steveninck & Anderson (1998) metabolic cost neural information, Nature Neuroscience 1(1):36–41. rl_update_freq Integer. Apply RL update every many ticks (default 1L). lamarckian Logical. TRUE rl_mode "none", within-lifetime RL-updated brain weights written back parent's genome meiosis. Offspring therefore directly inherit learned solution rather rediscovering unmodified starting genome (Darwinian path). distinct epigenetics: epigenetics inherits methylation marks record loci canalized; Lamarckian inheritance copies actual learned weight values heritable material. can active simultaneously (default FALSE). Reference: Baldwin (1896) American Naturalist 30:441–451; Weismann (1892) Das Keimplasma; Jablonka & Lamb (2005) Evolution Four Dimensions, MIT Press. phenotypic_plasticity Logical. Enable heritable plasticity trait (default FALSE). Gates DeWitt & Scheiner 2004 pathway. plasticity_init_mean Numeric. Starting mean plasticity trait (default 0.3). plasticity_mutation_sd Numeric. Per-offspring mutation SD plasticity trait (default 0.05). plasticity_min, plasticity_max Numeric clamps heritable plasticity trait (default 0 / 1). plasticity_sense_radius Integer. Sensory-radius bonus plasticity high (default 1L). Unused plasticity = 0.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: epigenetics = TRUE, agent carries methylome: Boolean vector length genome. element records whether corresponding locus methylated. Within lifetime, learning events can methylate loci (epigenetic_learning_coupling). reproduction, fraction parent's methylation pattern inherited offspring (epigenetic_inheritance). BNN brains, methylation locus shrinks prior sigma_i (canalization – agent becomes less plastic weight). implements transgenerational epigenetic inheritance (TEI) described Jablonka & Lamb (2005). epigenetics Logical. Enable epigenetic methylation TEI (default FALSE). Reference: Jablonka & Lamb (2005) Evolution Four Dimensions, MIT Press. epigenetic_learning_coupling Numeric [0, 1]. Probability within-lifetime learning event methylates corresponding locus (default 0.10). epigenetic_inheritance Numeric [0, 1]. Fraction parent's methylation marks inherited offspring (default 0.50). remaining marks reset (demethylated) offspring. epigenetic_effect_size Numeric. Factor methylation reduces prior sigma (BNN) mutational variance locus (brain types) (default 0.20). methylation_rate Numeric. Spontaneous methylation probability per locus per tick (default 0.001). demethylation_rate Numeric. Spontaneous demethylation probability per methylated locus per tick (default 0.002).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: n_predators_init Integer. Number predators tick 0 (default 0L). Predators evolving brains ("ann" default) co-evolve prey. predator_energy_init Numeric. Starting energy predators (default 150). predator_attack_strength Numeric. Energy damage dealt prey per successful predator attack (default 40). Named predator_attack_cost docs 0.5.6. predator_energy_gain Numeric. Energy predator gains per successful kill (default 30). Named predator_kill_gain docs 0.5.6. predator_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD predator brain weights (default 0.1). predator_max_agents Integer. Hard cap predator population (default 50L). Named max_predators docs 0.5.6. predator_max_age Integer. Predator-specific maximum age (default 100L — predators typically outlive prey: owl > mouse). Added 0.5.6. predator_min_repro_energy Numeric. Energy threshold predator reproduction (default 200). predator_min_repro_age Integer. Minimum predator age reproduction (default 20L). predator_move_energy Numeric. Energy deducted per predator move (default 1.0). predator_live_energy Numeric. Per-tick passive energy cost live predator (default 0.5). predator_sense_graded Logical. TRUE (default, 0.4.2), prey's predator sensory input distance d 1/(d+1) (closer predators produce stronger signal). FALSE, falls back pre-0.4.2 binary presence signal. effect n_predators_init = 0.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: life_history Character. "iteroparous" (default) "semelparous". Semelparous agents die immediately reproducing. Reference: Stearns (1992) Evolution Life Histories, Oxford . max_age Integer. Maximum lifespan ticks; agents die age regardless energy (default 200L). hard cap applies senescence_rate == 0; Gompertz senescence active, late-life mortality governed stochastic curve instead (0.4.2 behaviour). Set Inf disable. senescence_rate Numeric [0, 1]. Gompertz mortality rate; per-tick death probability scales exp(senescence_rate * age) (default 0 = senescence). > 0, supersedes max_age hard cap (0.4.2). Reference: Gompertz (1825) nature function expressive law human mortality, Philosophical Transactions Royal Society 115:513–583. allee_threshold Integer. Minimum local density reproduction (default 0L = Allee effects ). > 0, agents require least many conspecifics Moore neighbourhood reproduce. Reference: Allee (1931) Animal Aggregations, University Chicago Press.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: body_size_evolution Logical. Enable heritable body size trait (default FALSE). Body size scales metabolic costs (larger = expensive), foraging capacity (larger = grass per tick), energy storage (larger = higher cap). Reference size 1.0 (effect). Reference: Kleiber, M. (1947) Body size metabolic rate, Physiological Reviews 27(4):511–541. body_size_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean body size (default 1.0). body_size_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD body size (default 0.08). body_size_min Numeric. Minimum body size (default 0.3). body_size_max Numeric. Maximum body size (default 3.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: brain_size_evolution Logical. Enable heritable brain size trait (default FALSE). Implements parental provisioning hypothesis: brain size metabolically costly (expensive brain hypothesis) yet confers cognitive foraging advantage. bootstrapping problem — large-brained offspring pay metabolic cost birth cognitive advantage can offset — means brain size can evolve parental provisioning (parental_care = TRUE) buffers infancy energy deficit. References: van Schaik et al. (2023) PLoS Biology 21(5):e3002064; Griesser et al. (2023) PNAS 120(31):e2301005120; Song et al. (2025) PNAS 122(8):e2412783122. brain_size_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean brain size (default 1.0, reference = effect). brain_size_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD brain size (default 0.05). brain_size_min Numeric. Minimum brain size (default 0.1). brain_size_max Numeric. Maximum brain size (default 3.0). brain_size_cost_scale Numeric. Multiplier metabolic surcharge per unit brain_size - 1.0. Higher values steepen cost curve make bootstrapping problem harder (default 1.0). brain_size_sensing_exponent Numeric. Power applied brain_size scaling grass sensing inputs. brain_size^exponent determines sensing multiplier. Exponent 0 = sensing effect; exponent 1.0 = linear scaling; default 0.3 gives gentle sublinear boost (e.g. brain_size = 2.0 → 1.23× multiplier). effect brain_size_evolution = FALSE.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: metabolic_rate_evolution Logical. Enable heritable metabolic rate (default FALSE). Scales move_cost idle_cost. metabolic_rate_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean metabolic rate (default 1.0). metabolic_rate_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD (default 0.05). metabolic_rate_min Numeric. Minimum (default 0.1). metabolic_rate_max Numeric. Maximum (default 5.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: aging_rate_evolution Logical. Enable heritable aging rate (default FALSE). Scales Gompertz senescence exponent. aging_rate_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean aging rate (default 1.0). aging_rate_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD (default 0.05). aging_rate_min Numeric. Minimum (default 0.01). aging_rate_max Numeric. Maximum (default 10.0). senescence_shape Numeric > 0. Curvature exponent age 2-parameter Gompertz hazard \(p = 1 - \exp(-r \cdot \exp(r \cdot \mathrm{age}^{\mathrm{shape}}))\), \(r = \mathrm{senescence\_rate} \times \mathrm{aging\_rate}\). Default 1.0 = classic Gompertz (Gompertz 1825). Values > 1 accelerate senescence late life; values < 1 give late-life mortality plateau (Vaupel et al. 1998). matters senescence_rate > 0. max_age_scales_with_metabolism Logical. TRUE, effective lifespan scales inversely metabolic rate (pace--life syndrome, Réale et al. 2010). Default FALSE. Added 0.4.0 Tier 2.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: immune_evolution Logical. Enable heritable immune strength trait (default FALSE). immune_strength_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean (default 0.3). immune_strength_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD (default 0.05). immune_strength_min Numeric. Minimum (default 0.0). immune_strength_max Numeric. Maximum (default 1.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: disease Logical. Enable SIR disease dynamics (default FALSE). Reference: Kermack & McKendrick (1927) Contributions mathematical theory epidemics, Proceedings Royal Society 115(772):700–721. disease_seed_prob Numeric [0, 1]. Probability agent initially infected tick 1 (default 0.01). transmission_prob Numeric [0, 1]. Probability transmission susceptible neighbour per infected-susceptible adjacent pair per tick (default 0.1). Effective transmission = transmission_prob * (1 - receiver's immune_strength). disease_duration Integer. Ticks agent remains infectious (default 10L). immune_duration Integer. Ticks immunity recovery (default 20L). , agent returns Susceptible. disease_energy_cost Numeric. Energy deducted per tick infected (default 5). disease_death_prob Numeric [0, 1]. Per-tick probability death infected (default 0.02). Scaled (1 - immune_strength) immune_evolution = TRUE.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: kin_selection Logical. Enable kin altruism (default FALSE). Reference: Hamilton (1964) genetical evolution social behaviour & II, Journal Theoretical Biology 7:1–52. kin_altruism_cost Numeric. Energy transferred donor (default 2.0). Hamilton's rule requires benefit * r > cost. kin_altruism_benefit Numeric. Energy received recipient (default 10.0). kin_altruism_r_min Numeric. Minimum pedigree relatedness r required trigger altruism (default 0.25 = half-siblings). kin_altruism_min_donor_energy Numeric. Donor must least energy donate (default 50).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: cooperation_evolution Logical. Enable heritable cooperation level (default FALSE). Uses spatial public goods game (Nowak & May 1992). Reference: Nowak & May (1992) Evolutionary games spatial chaos, Nature 359:826–829. cooperation_multiplier Numeric. Payoff multiplier M public goods game (default 2.0). cooperator pays cost C; group receives M * sum(contributions) / n_local. cooperation selectively favoured, M must exceed group size. cooperation_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean cooperation level (default 0.5). cooperation_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD (default 0.05). cooperation_cost Numeric. Per-tick energy cost paid cooperator (default 1.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: dispersal_evolution Logical. Enable heritable natal dispersal (default FALSE). agent carries dispersal_tendency trait (per-tick probability moving away birthplace). Reduces inbreeding kin competition. Reference: Ronce, O. (2007) feel like rolling stone? Annual Review Ecology, Evolution, Systematics 38:231–253. dispersal_cost Numeric. Energy cost per dispersal step (default 2.0). Agents energy <= 2 x dispersal_cost disperse. dispersal_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean dispersal tendency (default 0.1). dispersal_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD dispersal tendency (default 0.02). dispersal_min Numeric. Minimum dispersal tendency (default 0.0). dispersal_max Numeric. Maximum dispersal tendency (default 0.5).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: parental_care Logical. Enable altricial parental care (default FALSE). Offspring remain parent graduation. juvenile_independence_age Integer. Tick age offspring leaves parental care (default 10L). Replaces pre-0.4.0 care_duration spec. juvenile_independence_energy Numeric. Energy level offspring leaves parental care early (default 50.0). feeding_rate Numeric. Energy transferred per tick carrying parent offspring (default 5.0). care_cost_per_tick Numeric. Energy cost paid parent per tick active care (default 1.0). Named care_cost_per_offspring docs 0.5.6. max_clutch_size Integer. Maximum offspring per reproductive event (default 1L). neonatal_foraging_deficit Numeric [0, 1]. Reduction foraging efficiency (effective max_bite) newborns first neonatal_deficit_duration ticks life (default 0.0). Creates selection gradient parental provisioning — unprovisioned newborns forage effectively, provisioned ones fed parent via feeding_rate. Reference: Aiello & Wheeler (1995); Isler & van Schaik (2009) expensive brain hypothesis. neonatal_deficit_duration Integer. many ticks neonatal foraging deficit applies (default 10L). parental_investment_evolution Logical. Enable Trivers 1972 quality-quantity trade-(default FALSE). TRUE, female (focal agent) bears female_investment per-offspring cost male bears 1 - female_investment; offspring birth energy also scales 2 * female_investment. See inst/julia/src/reproduce.jl implementation. female_investment Numeric [0, 1]. Fraction offspring energy cost paid mother (default 1.0; 0.5 = equal). Added 0.4.0 Tier 3. male_repro_cost Numeric. Per-offspring energy cost paid father female_investment < 1 (default 0.0). cooperative_breeding Logical. Enable helper---nest dynamics (default FALSE). helper_tendency_init_mean Numeric [0, 1]. Heritable willingness help (default 0.2). helper_tendency_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD helper trait (default 0.05). helper_transfer Numeric. Energy transferred per helping event (default 3.0). helper_kin_threshold Numeric. Minimum relatedness helping attempt (default 0.125 = half-siblings). helper_min_energy Numeric. Minimum helper energy transfer refused (default 60.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: Lack (1947) r/K clutch-size evolution. clutch_size_evolution Logical. Enable heritable clutch size (default FALSE). clutch_size_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean clutch size (default 1.0). clutch_size_min, clutch_size_max Integer clamps (default 1L / 5L). clutch_size_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD (default 0.3).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: Rosenberg (2001), Foster (2007): low energy ("stress"), mutation rate rises transiently. Controlled three specs: stress_hypermutation Logical. Enable stress-induced hypermutation (default FALSE). stress_threshold Numeric. Energy agent considered "stressed" (default 20.0). Note: stress hypermutation fires reproduction time, per-tick. Reproduction requires energy >= min_repro_energy (default 120), stress_threshold must GREATER min_repro_energy hypermutation ever trigger. defaults (threshold = 20, min_repro = 120), module structurally silent — set stress_threshold > 120 stress-mutation actually fire reproduction events. stress_mutation_multiplier Numeric. Multiplier applied mutation_sd reproduction parent's energy stress_threshold (default 5.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: signal_dims Integer. Number signal dimensions (default 0L = ). > 0, agent evolves heritable signal vector preference vector; mates chosen proximity signal preference. signal_cost Numeric. Energy cost per unit signal magnitude per tick (default 0.1). Indirect handicap cost — high-signal agents lose energy starve faster. Often masked drift realistic parameters; see also signal_cost_mortality direct viability cost. Reference: Zahavi (1975) Mate selection – selection handicap, Journal Theoretical Biology 53(1):205–214. signal_cost_mortality Numeric [0, 1]. Direct per-tick mortality probability scaling signal magnitude (default 0.0 = ). Implements Zahavi handicap Fuller, Houle & Travis (2005) β_Sv viability-selection gradient — right cost mechanism distinguishing Fisher runaway sensory bias Zahavi handicap. Distinct signal_cost (drains energy). References: Grafen (1990) Biological signals handicaps, JTB 144:517–546. Fuller, Houle & Travis (2005) Sensory bias explanation evolution mate preferences, Nat 166:437–446. Added 0.6.3. preference_bias_target Numeric vector length signal_dims, NULL (default). fixed target vector agent preferences pulled toward tick. Installs pre-existing sensory bias — Ryan (1990) sensory- exploitation mechanism β_N leg Fuller, Houle & Travis (2005). Signals preference-based mate choice evolve match target generations. Active preference_bias_strength > 0 signal_dims > 0. Added 0.6.5. preference_bias_strength Numeric [0, 1]. Per-tick pull strength κ preference vector toward preference_bias_target (default 0.0 = , opt-). update preference[] ← (1 - κ) × preference[] + κ × target[], clamped [-1, 1]. κ = 0.01 weak bias; 0.1 strong. References: Ryan (1990) Oxford Surveys Evolutionary Biology 7:157–195; Endler & Basolo (1998) TREE 13:415–420. Added 0.6.5. signal_toxicity_coupling Numeric [0, 1]. Strength aposematic pleiotropy first signal dimension heritable toxicity trait. 0 (default) signal toxicity evolve independently; 1, signal[1] locked agent's toxicity value predators can learn reliable honest aposematic signal. Required close Bates (1862) / Müller (1879) feedback loop clade. Active mimicry = TRUE signal_dims > 0. Added 0.4.4. signal_drift_sd Numeric. Neutral drift SD applied per-tick agent's signal vector (default 0). 0 signal fixed birth; > 0 lets signals drift within-lifetime. signal_evolution_drift Numeric. Inter-generational drift SD inherited signal (default 0). Separate sensory mutation rate. signal_memory_rate Numeric. Rate predator memory updates toward observed prey signals (default 0.1). Relevant mimicry = TRUE predators present. mate_choice_mode Character. Mate-choice rule: "preference" (default, score -||preference - candidate signal||^2 sample softmax), "random" (uniform random among eligible neighbours), "highest_signal" (score signal magnitude). Wired 0.6.4; field latent stub signal_dims > 0 always produced hard argmax preference-distance regardless mode. See NEWS.md 0.6.4 migration notes. mate_choice_strength Numeric [0, 1]. Softmax temperature parameter (default 1.0, greedy argmax). 1, choice hard argmax mode-specific score (preserves pre-0.6.4 observed behaviour); 0, uniform random regardless mode; intermediate values sample softmax temperature 1 / mate_choice_strength. Ignored mate_choice_mode = "random".

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: coevolving_parasites Logical. Enable genotype-matched virulence module (default FALSE). Reference: Hamilton, W.D. (1980) Sex versus non-sex versus parasite. Oikos 35:282–290. parasite_match_mode Character. One "auto" (default), "continuous", "discrete". Continuous mode (0.5.0) uses Euclidean distance signal vector tracks host population centroid lag — mean-tracking variant reproduce canonical Red Queen. Discrete mode (0.5.1) uses Hamming distance binary-allele parasite_haplotype trait Mendelian inheritance — canonical Hamilton mechanism. "auto" picks discrete n_parasite_loci > 0, else continuous. parasite_virulence_rate Numeric [0, 1]. Rate collective parasite genotype tracks host majority tick (default 0.1 ≈ 10-tick lag). parasite_pressure Numeric. Maximum per-tick energy drain parasite infection, applied hosts exactly matching parasite haplotype (default 0.5). parasite_distance_scale Numeric. Continuous-mode Gaussian falloff scale (default 1.0; ignored discrete mode). n_parasite_loci Integer. Number binary loci heritable parasite_haplotype trait (default 0 = continuous mode ). > 0, agent carries Vector{Int32} length, inherited Mendelian-style diploid, clonal + per-locus mutation haploid. 0.5.1. parasite_mutation_rate Numeric [0, 1]. Per-locus mutation rate (allele flip) inheritance (default 0.01). parasite_discrete_exponent Numeric. Exponent controlling Hamming-distance falloff: penalty = pressure * ((n_loci - hamming) / n_loci)^exp (default 4.0). Higher exponents concentrate pressure near-matching hosts let mismatched hosts escape cleanly.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: mimicry Logical. Enable heritable toxicity trait predator signal learning (default FALSE). Implements Mullerian Batesian mimicry via Rescorla-Wagner learning predators. Reference: Rescorla & Wagner (1972) theory Pavlovian conditioning, Classical Conditioning II, Appleton-Century-Crofts, pp 64–99. batesian_mimicry Logical. Enable Batesian mimicry branch (default FALSE): palatable prey (toxicity = 0) can exploit predator's learned aversion toxic signals. Predator-betrayal decay prevents runaway cheating. Active mimicry = TRUE. toxicity_init_mean Numeric. Starting toxicity (default 0). toxicity_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD toxicity trait (default 0.05). toxicity_cost_per_tick Numeric. Per-tick energy cost paid agents toxicity > 0 (default 2.0; Zahavi handicap). toxin_dose Numeric. Damage dealt attacker per unit prey toxicity (default 2.0). avoid_threshold Numeric. Predator-memory value predator chooses avoid prey (default 0.3).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: niche_construction Logical. Enable shelter building (default FALSE). Shelters reduce predation slow grass regrowth. Reference: Odling-Smee, Laland & Feldman (2003) Niche Construction: Neglected Process Evolution, Princeton . shelter_build_prob Numeric [0, 1]. Probability building shelter unit per tick eligible (default 0.1). shelter_max_depth Integer. Maximum shelter depth per cell (default 5L). shelter_min_energy Numeric. Minimum energy required build (default 80). shelter_decay_prob Numeric [0, 1]. Per-tick probability shelter unit decays (default 0.05). shelter_occupancy_bonus Numeric. Energy bonus per tick per unit shelter depth agents occupying sheltered cell (default 0, meaning shelters feed occupants). Heritable niche-construction amplifier Odling-Smee et al. 2003 sense. Added 0.3.0.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: scavenging Logical. Enable carrion dynamics (default FALSE). Dead agents deposit body mass carrion; scavengers consume . carrion_fraction Numeric [0, 1]. Fraction energy_init deposited carrion upon agent death (default 0.5). Uses energy_init agent$energy death, body mass change starvation. carrion_decay_rate Numeric [0, 1]. Per-tick fraction carrion decomposes (default 0.1). carrion_eat_gain Numeric. Energy gained per unit carrion consumed (default 3). carrion_transmission_prob Numeric [0, 1]. Probability scavenger feeding infected carrion contracts disease (default 0; active disease = TRUE scavenging = TRUE). Cross-module fomite-transmission pathway.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: group_defense Logical. Enable dilution-effect predator avoidance (default FALSE). Per-agent predation probability decreases local group size. Reference: Foster & Treherne (1981) Evidence dilution effect selfish herd fish predation marine insect, Nature 293:466–467. group_defense_radius Integer. Moore radius counting neighbours computing per-prey dilution (default 2L = 5×5 neighbourhood). group_defense_strength Numeric [0, 1]. Maximum fraction predator attack success reduced maximum grouping (default 0.3). 2026-04-17 audit found population-level effect inverts Hamilton 1971 tested values — see dev/audit/fidelity/group_defense_strength_sweep.R.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: habitat_preference_evolution Logical. Enable heritable habitat preference (default FALSE). Preference encodes optimal local grass density; agents attracted cells matching preference. Reference: Fretwell & Lucas (1970) territorial behavior factors influencing habitat distribution birds, Acta Biotheoretica 19(1):16–36. habitat_preference_init_mean Numeric [-1, 1]. Initial mean preference (default 0 — preference). Negative values = prefer low-grass cells; positive = prefer high-grass. habitat_preference_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD preference trait (default 0.05). habitat_preference_min, habitat_preference_max Numeric clamps (default -1 / 1). habitat_preference_strength Numeric [0, ∞). Scales probability agent attempts preference-directed move per tick (default 0.5). 2026-04-17 audit found default drift floor; strength = 2.0 required detectable Fretwell-Lucas signal 5 seeds. habitat_move_cost Numeric. Energy cost preference-directed move (default 0, meaning move free).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: seasonal_amplitude Numeric. Amplitude sinusoidal grass_rate modulation (default 0 = ). grass_rate_t = grass_rate * (1 + seasonal_amplitude * sin(2 * pi * t / season_length)). season_length Integer. Period seasonal cycle ticks (default 100L). Named seasonal_period docs 0.5.6. winter_death_prob Numeric [0, 1]. Per-tick death probability low phase seasonal cycle (default 0). engages seasonal_amplitude > 0.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: speciation Logical. Enable reproductive isolation based genome distance (default FALSE). Agents mate conspecifics (genome distance < isolation_threshold). Reference: Gavrilets (2004) Fitness Landscapes Origin Species, Princeton . isolation_threshold Numeric. Maximum genome distance successful mating (default 0.45). Species inferred hierarchical clustering genome distances logging tick. speciation_cluster_interval Integer. Ticks species- count recomputations (default 10L). Reducing improves temporal resolution n_species log CPU cost.

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: social_learning Logical. Enable copying output-layer weights successful neighbours (default FALSE). Implements prestige bias. Reference: Laland (2004) Social learning strategies, Learning Behavior 32(1):4–14. social_learning_freq Integer. Apply social learning every many ticks (default 10L). social_learning_rate Numeric. Fraction neighbour weights copied (default 0.1).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: Adds shrub canopy resource layers top ground grass. Canopy access requires wing_size >= canopy_threshold (heritable). Designed break standard brain-size x longevity correlation. Reference: Liedtke & Fromhage (2019); Isbell (2006). complex_landscape Logical. Enable multi-layer resources (default FALSE). shrub_density Numeric [0, 1]. Initial fraction cells shrub resource (default 0.3). shrub_growth_rate Numeric. Per-tick fractional regrowth shrubs (default 0.03). shrub_energy Numeric. Maximum energy shrub cell provides (default 20.0). canopy_density Numeric [0, 1]. Initial canopy coverage (default 0.15; trees sparse). canopy_growth_rate Numeric. Per-tick fractional regrowth canopy (default 0.005; trees grow slowly). canopy_energy Numeric. Maximum energy canopy cell provides (default 50.0; high energy density aerial specialists). canopy_threshold Numeric. Minimum wing_size needed canopy access (default 0.6). wing_size_init_mean Numeric. Initial mean wing_size (default 0.0; ground-bound founders). wing_size_mutation_sd Numeric. Mutation SD wing_size (default 0.05). wing_size_min Numeric. Minimum wing size (default 0.0). wing_size_max Numeric. Maximum wing size (default 1.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: Implements assortative mating invasion range front, causing dispersal-enhancing alleles accumulate frontier without requiring fitness advantage sedentary conspecifics. Reference: Shine et al. (2011) evolutionary process assembles phenotypes space rather time. PNAS 108:5708–5711. Requires dispersal_evolution = TRUE effect. spatial_sorting Logical. Enable range-front mate preference high-dispersal partners (default FALSE). sorting_front_threshold Numeric [0, 1]. Fraction maximum range radius defines "front" (default 0.75; outermost 25%). sorting_mating_boost Numeric. Multiplier applied dispersal_tendency scoring mates front (default 3.0).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: IFfolk = offspring + sum(r x relative's offspring). Agents transfer energy energy-depleted kin. Optional parliament suppression penalises defectors cooperative populations. Reference: Fromhage & Jennions (2019) strategic reference gene. Proc R Soc B 286:20190459. doi:10.1098/rspb.2019.0459 iffolk_selection Logical. Enable IFfolk energy transfers (default FALSE). iffolk_r_min Numeric. Minimum relatedness coefficient relative count (default 0.125 = cousin). iffolk_radius Integer. Spatial search radius relatives grid cells (default 5L). iffolk_transfer Numeric. Maximum energy transferred per altruistic act (default 3.0). iffolk_min_energy Numeric. Minimum donor energy required transfer (default 60.0). parliament_suppression Logical. Enable parliament--genes penalty defectors (negative helper_tendency) surrounded cooperators (default FALSE). parliament_cost Numeric. Energy cost per tick defectors cooperative neighbourhoods (default 0.5).

what: Fixed patch (stable fitness peak — Baldwin Effect demonstration)
title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: Places one grid cells whose grass value reset fixed_patch_value every grow_grass! call, creating permanent high-value resource never depletes. gives natural selection stable fitness peak: agents whose genome encodes fixed policy navigating patch always outperform agents must re-discover exploration lifetime. result genetic assimilation patch-navigation strategy — computational Baldwin Effect described Hinton \& Nowlan (1987). Without module, foraging fitness landscape shifts continuously (stochastic grass, population density feedbacks) exploration remains evolutionarily stable strategy (mean_prior_sigma rises ceiling). Reference: Hinton, G.E. \& Nowlan, S.J. (1987) learning can guide evolution. Complex Systems 1(3):495–502. fixed_patch Logical. Enable stable resource patch (default FALSE). fixed_patch_value Numeric. Grass value maintained patch cells tick (default 5.0, equal grass_max). Values grass_max permitted — cap grow_grass! apply patch uses direct assignment. fixed_patch_x Integer NA_integer_. Column index patch centre (1-indexed). NA_integer_ resolves grid centre column (default). fixed_patch_y Integer NA_integer_. Row index patch centre (1-indexed). NA_integer_ resolves grid centre row (default). fixed_patch_radius Integer. Chebyshev radius patch: 0L = single cell; 1L = 3×3 block; 2L = 5×5 block (default 0L).

title: Default simulation parameters for clade — default_specs
text: log_freq Integer. Log population-level statistics every many ticks (default 1L; set higher reduce memory use long runs). log_genomes Logical. Log flattened genome vectors get_genome_data()$genomes log tick (default FALSE; memory intensive large populations). random_seed Integer NA_integer_. Seed Julia's RNG. NA_integer_ uses random seed (default).

what: Estimate narrow-sense heritability from a logged trait time-series — estimate_heritability
title: Estimate narrow-sense heritability from a logged trait time-series — estimate_heritability
text: estimate_heritability() returns coarse estimate narrow-sense heritability (\(h^2\)) quantitative trait logged per tick run_alife(). estimator lag-1 temporal autocorrelation population mean, $$\hat{h}^2 \approx \mathrm{cor}(\bar{z}_t,\, \bar{z}_{t+1}),$$ used proxy parent-offspring regression (Falconer & Mackay 1996, ch. 10). proxy reasonable () trait directional stabilising selection, (ii) generation overlap moderate, (iii) logging interval short relative generation time. exact quantitative-genetic estimate reported one. exact estimate requires parent-offspring pairs, clade currently log.

title: Estimate narrow-sense heritability from a logged trait time-series — estimate_heritability
code: estimate_heritability(run_data, trait = "body_size")

title: Estimate narrow-sense heritability from a logged trait time-series — estimate_heritability
text: run_data list returned get_run_data() (must contain $ticks column paste0("mean_", trait)). trait Character. trait name (without mean_ prefix). Defaults "body_size". column form mean_<trait> run_data$ticks supported (e.g. "immune_strength", "metabolic_rate", "learning_rate").

title: Estimate narrow-sense heritability from a logged trait time-series — estimate_heritability
text: list components: $h2 Numeric. Lag-1 autocorrelation mean_<trait>. Returns NA_real_ series fewer three usable values zero variance. $method Character constant "lag1_autocorrelation". $trait trait name supplied caller. $n Integer. Number paired observations used. $note Character. reminder proxy exact estimate requires parent-offspring logging.

title: Estimate narrow-sense heritability from a logged trait time-series — estimate_heritability
text: Falconer, D.S. & Mackay, T.F.C. (1996) Introduction Quantitative Genetics, 4th ed. Longman, Harlow.

title: Estimate narrow-sense heritability from a logged trait time-series — estimate_heritability
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ env  <- run_alife(default_specs()) rd   <- get_run_data(env) estimate_heritability(rd, trait = "body_size") estimate_heritability(rd, trait = "immune_strength") } # }

what: Fast-generation specs for evolutionary scenarios — fast_specs
title: Fast-generation specs for evolutionary scenarios — fast_specs
text: Returns default_specs() calibrated fast generational turnover — 66 generations 2000 ticks (vs ~2.6 defaults). Use preset whenever scenario tests prediction trait evolution across generations (plasticity, Baldwin effect, mimicry, mating systems, dispersal evolution, etc.).

title: Fast-generation specs for evolutionary scenarios — fast_specs
code: fast_specs()

title: Fast-generation specs for evolutionary scenarios — fast_specs
text: specs list calibrated fast evolutionary dynamics.

title: Fast-generation specs for evolutionary scenarios — fast_specs
text: timescale calibration based MATLAB ancestor's parameters (Bulitko 2023), ran ~40x faster generations clade's defaults agents started reproduction energy short minReproductionAge. See dev/docs/timescale-analysis.md full analysis. Key changes default_specs(): max_age 30L (vs 200L). Short lifespan forces generational turnover biologically realistic rate small organism (e.g., Drosophila, small rodent). min_repro_energy 60 (vs 120). Lower threshold means agents breed ~10 ticks foraging, ~100. min_repro_age 3L (vs 0L). Minimum maturation age prevents newborn-immediately-reproducing artefacts. grass_rate 0.20 (vs 0.05). Adequate food sustain viable population ~65 agents fast turnover. max_ticks 2000L. gen time ~30 ticks, gives ~66 generations — adequate evolutionary predictions. n_agents_init 80L. Moderate starting population. max_agents 400L. Room population growth. grid_rows, grid_cols 30L. Standard grid.

title: Full preset specs for publication-quality runs — full_specs

title: Full preset specs for publication-quality runs — full_specs
code: full_specs()

title: Full preset specs for publication-quality runs — full_specs
text: specs list identical default_specs() except: n_agents_init 200L max_ticks 1000L grid_rows, grid_cols 30L × 30L

title: Full preset specs for publication-quality runs — full_specs
text: Typical wall time: ~10–20 minutes per run (Julia warm-excluded).

what: Generate a procedural habitat map — generate_map
title: Generate a procedural habitat map — generate_map
text: Creates 0/1 wall matrix use run_alife(). Five landscape types available, ranging completely open structured corridor networks. Pass result prepare_map() validate dimensions use.

title: Generate a procedural habitat map — generate_map
code: generate_map(   type = "random_cluster",   grid_rows = 50L,   grid_cols = 50L,   p_wall = 0.3,   scale = 4,   corridor_width = 2L,   seed = NULL )

title: Generate a procedural habitat map — generate_map
text: type Character; one "open", "patchy", "random_cluster", "gaussian_field", "corridors". Default "random_cluster". grid_rows Integer; number grid rows. Default 50. grid_cols Integer; number grid columns. Default 50. p_wall Numeric (0, 1); target proportion wall cells. Default 0.3. Ignored "open". scale Positive numeric; spatial scale wall clustering (sigma Gaussian, autocorrelation range nlm_gaussianfield). Default 4. corridor_width Integer; open-corridor width cells ("corridors" ). Default 2. seed Integer NULL; random seed. Default NULL.

title: Generate a procedural habitat map — generate_map
text: integer matrix (0 = open, 1 = wall) dimensions grid_rows x grid_cols. Assign specs$wall_map calling run_alife(), pass prepare_map() first.

title: Generate a procedural habitat map — generate_map
text: Types "random_cluster" "gaussian_field" use NLMR package installed; otherwise pure-R Gaussian-smoothed fallback used. NLMR removed CRAN; install ropensci/NLMR (e.g. remotes::install_github("ropensci/NLMR")) want higher-quality neutral-landscape generators.

title: Generate a procedural habitat map — generate_map
code: map <- generate_map("random_cluster", grid_rows = 20L, grid_cols = 20L,                     p_wall = 0.25, seed = 1L) mean(map == 0L)  # fraction open #> [1] 0.75

title: Extract per-tick genome data (allele frequencies, diversity, FST) — get_genome_data
text: list components: $genomes list matrices (one per logged tick). matrix one row per agent one column per genome position. NULL specs$log_genomes = FALSE. $heterozygosity Reserved field — currently returns numeric(0L). Future versions compute mean per-locus heterozygosity across logged ticks. $fst Reserved field — currently returns numeric(0L). Future versions compute per-tick FST (Weir & Cockerham 1984) species speciation = TRUE.
