what: Generate a factorial grid of specs — grid_specs
title: Generate a factorial grid of specs — grid_specs
text: Produces list specs objects, one per combination supplied parameter values. Every field inherited base. Useful systematic parameter-space exploration batch_alife().

title: Generate a factorial grid of specs — grid_specs
code: grid_specs(base, ..., seed_from = 1L)

title: Generate a factorial grid of specs — grid_specs
text: base specs list (default_specs(), fast_specs(), etc.) use template combination. ... Named vectors / lists candidate values parameter. example, grass_rate = c(0.1, 0.2, 0.3), mutation_sd = c(0.02, 0.05) generates 3 × 2 = 6-cell grid. String-typed parameters can also passed (e.g. life_history = c("iteroparous", "semelparous")). seed_from Integer NULL. provided, overrides cell's random_seed seed_from + 0L, seed_from + 1L, ... grid run reproducible. Default 1L.

title: Generate a factorial grid of specs — grid_specs
text: named list specs. Names encode parameter values, e.g. "grass_rate=0.1;mutation_sd=0.02".

title: Generate a factorial grid of specs — grid_specs
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ base <- fast_specs() specs_list <- grid_specs(base,                          grass_rate   = c(0.1, 0.2, 0.3),                          mutation_sd  = c(0.02, 0.05)) results <- batch_alife(specs_list, n_cores = 6L) } # }

what: Compute contrast tests from a hypothesis_sweep — hypothesis_report
title: Compute contrast tests from a hypothesis_sweep — hypothesis_report
text: hypothesis_report() produces t-tests (Welch, two-sample) named pairwise contrasts drawn hypothesis_sweep() result. conventional behavioural-ecology audit pattern name contrast direction--effect question ("parental care reduce population variance?") report Δ ± SE t across seeds.

title: Compute contrast tests from a hypothesis_sweep — hypothesis_report
code: hypothesis_report(sweep, contrasts, metric = NULL)

title: Compute contrast tests from a hypothesis_sweep — hypothesis_report
text: sweep hypothesis_sweep object hypothesis_sweep(). contrasts Named list pairwise contrasts. element length-two character vector c(reference, test) naming conditions present sweep$conditions. metric Character. Name metric column sweep$runs test. Defaults first metric.

title: Compute contrast tests from a hypothesis_sweep — hypothesis_report
text: S3 "hypothesis_report" object — list table (data frame contrast statistics) metric (metric name). print() method renders table.

title: Compute contrast tests from a hypothesis_sweep — hypothesis_report
text: contrast length-two character vector specifying c(reference_condition, test_condition). reported delta mean(test) − mean(reference) chosen metric; interpretation sign left caller. verdict column uses 2σ threshold convention shared across clade's fidelity audits: |t| >= 2 → PASS, 1.5 <= |t| < 2 → marginal, otherwise → null. screening heuristic, formal hypothesis test — report underlying statistic publications.

title: Compute contrast tests from a hypothesis_sweep — hypothesis_report
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ sweep <- hypothesis_sweep(...) hypothesis_report(sweep,                   contrasts = list(                     food_effect = c("low", "high"),                     cost_under_stress = c("cost0_stress", "cost1_stress")                   ),                   metric = "final_n") } # }

what: Sweep hypothesis conditions across seeds and compute per-run metrics — hypothesis_sweep
title: Sweep hypothesis conditions across seeds and compute per-run metrics — hypothesis_sweep
text: hypothesis_sweep() researcher-facing wrapper common simulation workflow surfaced repeatedly clade's fidelity audits: define base spec, vary small number parameters across several conditions, replicate condition across seeds, collect summary metrics run's tick log.

title: Sweep hypothesis conditions across seeds and compute per-run metrics — hypothesis_sweep
code: hypothesis_sweep(   base_specs,   conditions,   seeds = 1:8,   metrics = NULL,   n_cores = 1L,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Sweep hypothesis conditions across seeds and compute per-run metrics — hypothesis_sweep
text: base_specs specs list default_specs(), fast_specs(), realistic_specs(), etc. Supplies template run's spec derived. conditions Named list conditions. element named list parameter overrides, e.g. list(cost_low = list(signal_cost = 0.0, grass_rate = 0.2)). names appear output condition column. seeds Integer vector random seeds (default 1:8). condition replicated across seeds. metrics Named list functions. function takes ticks tibble returned get_run_data() returns length-one scalar (numeric logical). Defaults collect final_n (mean n_agents last 500 ticks) crashed (logical — whether n_agents fell 10 run end). n_cores Integer. Passed batch_alife(). Set close possible length(conditions) * length(seeds) keep worker one run (subject machine's compute limits — see CLAUDE.md per-machine caps). verbose Logical. Passed batch_alife() (default FALSE).

title: Sweep hypothesis conditions across seeds and compute per-run metrics — hypothesis_sweep
text: S3 object class "hypothesis_sweep" — list : runs: tibble one row per run, columns condition, seed, plus one column per metric. conditions: input conditions list (re-referencing). metrics: input metrics list. base_specs: input base specs. seeds: seeds vector. elapsed: difftime batch run. object print() method summarises per-condition means ± SE across seeds.

title: Sweep hypothesis conditions across seeds and compute per-run metrics — hypothesis_sweep
text: condition named list parameter overrides applied top base_specs. Conditions crossed seeds produce length(conditions) * length(seeds) runs, dispatched via batch_alife(). Metrics evaluated run's get_run_data(env)$ticks tibble (see get_run_data()) must return scalar length-one logical. Common choices — mean last N ticks, peak value, end--run value — illustrated examples.

title: Sweep hypothesis conditions across seeds and compute per-run metrics — hypothesis_sweep
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Example: how does grass_rate affect equilibrium population? specs <- fast_specs() specs$max_ticks <- 1000L  sweep <- hypothesis_sweep(   base_specs = specs,   conditions = list(     low  = list(grass_rate = 0.05),     mid  = list(grass_rate = 0.10),     high = list(grass_rate = 0.20)   ),   seeds = 1:8,   n_cores = 24L ) print(sweep)  # per-condition summary table  # Test a specific direction: hypothesis_report(sweep,                   contrasts = list(food_effect = c("low", "high")),                   metric = "final_n") } # }

Number layers brain$layers. $layer_sizes List integer vectors c(nrow, ncol) layer weight matrix. $n_weights Integer. Total number weight values across layers. $weight_mean Numeric. Mean weights. $weight_sd Numeric. Standard deviation weights. $weight_min Numeric. Minimum weight value. $weight_max Numeric. Maximum weight value. $sigma_mean, $sigma_sd, $sigma_min, $sigma_max Numeric. Statistics $sigma matrices (BNN brains ). $note Character. Present brain$layers unavailable; explains summary statistics absent.

what: Plot lifetime statistics of dead agents — plot_dead_agents
title: Plot lifetime statistics of dead agents — plot_dead_agents
text: Produces two panels summarising agents died run. left panel plots age death energy death, coloured body size (body-size evolution active) number offspring, sized offspring count. right panel shows bar chart cause--death counts (starvation, predation, disease, old age). Together reveal life-history strategies rewarded selection.

what: Plot genetic diversity over the run — plot_diversity
title: Plot genetic diversity over the run — plot_diversity
text: Draws trajectory mean pairwise genome distance (genetic diversity) , body-size evolution active, coefficient variation body size proxy phenotypic diversity. series scaled 0-1 range visual comparison. common pattern high diversity founders, selective sweep reduces diversity best foraging strategy spreads, partial recovery population niches. Permanent low diversity indicates genetic drift clonal selection; permanently high diversity indicates balancing selection frequency-dependent dynamics.

what: Plot module-specific metrics from a clade simulation run — plot_module_metrics
title: Plot module-specific metrics from a clade simulation run — plot_module_metrics
text: Detects optional simulation modules active run inspecting logged tick columns run_data$ticks, assembles six panels patchwork grid. panels modules produced non-zero data included: Predators n_predators – line plot predator population time. Species n_species – step plot species count; shown speciation active (max > 1). Traits Overlay mean_toxicity, mean_plasticity, mean_helper_tendency three coloured lines. Signals mean_signal_magnitude – line plot; shown signal evolution active (max > 0). Parental care n_juveniles – line plot juveniles care; shown max > 0. Mimicry n_toxic_attacks n_avoided_attacks – two lines showing attacks versus avoidance events; shown mimicry attacks occurred (max n_toxic_attacks > 0). fewer two panels active, single informative placeholder plot returned instead.

title: Plot module-specific metrics from a clade simulation run — plot_module_metrics

Must contain $ticks data frame. new module columns (n_predators, n_helpers, mean_signal_magnitude, mean_toxicity, mean_plasticity, mean_helper_tendency, n_species, n_juveniles, n_toxic_attacks, n_avoided_attacks) used present; absent columns silently skipped.

what: Visualise a neural genome as a weight heatmap — plot_weight_heatmap
title: Visualise a neural genome as a weight heatmap — plot_weight_heatmap
text: Renders weight matrix ANN-format brain (list layers element, layer $W $b) diverging blue-white-red tile heatmap. Blue = strong inhibitory connections, red = strong excitatory connections, white = near-zero weights. One panel per layer. Agents strong selection often show structured weight matrices (certain input--hidden connections consistently strong) compared near-random patterns newly initialised founders.

what: Realistic-scale specs for ecologically meaningful audits — realistic_specs
title: Realistic-scale specs for ecologically meaningful audits — realistic_specs
text: Returns fast_specs() scaled larger grid explicit predator age structure. Designed re-auditing scenarios default 30×30 grid small let genuine spatial dynamics (dispersal gradients, predator–prey waves, metapopulation structure) express .

title: Realistic-scale specs for ecologically meaningful audits — realistic_specs
code: realistic_specs()

title: Realistic-scale specs for ecologically meaningful audits — realistic_specs
text: specs list calibrated larger-grid, predator-aware audit runs.

title: Realistic-scale specs for ecologically meaningful audits — realistic_specs
text: Built top fast_specs() 2000-tick / 66-generation runs longest BNN kernel stays stable without trait drift degrading population.  5000-tick scale-tested produced systematic population decline t ≈ 1500 across seeds. fast_specs() settings preserved (max_age = 30, min_repro_energy = 60, min_repro_age = 3, grass_rate = 0.20). Additional changes: grid_rows, grid_cols 60L × 60L (4× default area). Enough room metapopulation structure, dispersal gradients, predator–prey waves. n_agents_init 150L. Right-sized post-boom equilibrium 60×60 grid; 5 tested seeds report viability_report() = viable. max_agents 1500L. Supports transient population peak (~280) equilibrium sets . max_ticks 2000L. 66 generations max_age = 30. predator_max_agents 150L. 3× default; room predator guild larger map. predator_max_age 60L. Predators outlive prey 2× (owl > mouse) — biologically realistic age structure predation engaged. Typical wall time: 30–60 seconds per run depending modules; 8 seeds parallel 16–32 PSOCK workers easily fit 200-core / 300-GB machine budget.

title: Run an evolutionary simulation — run_alife
text: run_alife() primary entry point clade. sends specs Julia , runs specs$max_ticks ticks entirely Julia, returns environment list containing final agent population logged statistics. Julia session started automatically first call.

title: Run an evolutionary simulation — run_alife
code: run_alife(specs = default_specs(), verbose = TRUE)

title: Run an evolutionary simulation — run_alife
text: specs named list simulation parameters, typically default_specs() modifications. parameters documented default_specs(). verbose Logical. Print progress messages (default TRUE). Pass FALSE batch runs testing.

title: Run an evolutionary simulation — run_alife
text: env list components: $agents list agent lists, one per surviving agent. $t Final tick number (equals specs$max_ticks). $specs specs list used run. $progress data frame per-tick logged statistics (get_run_data(env)$ticks). $deaths data frame per-death records (get_run_data(env)$deaths). $genome_log list per-tick genome matrices (non-NULL specs$log_genomes = TRUE).

title: Run an evolutionary simulation — run_alife
text: R-Julia boundary crossed per call regardless simulation length agent count. contrasts Rcpp-based simulators (including alifeR) data marshalled across R-C++ boundary every tick.

title: Run an evolutionary simulation — run_alife
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ specs <- default_specs() env   <- run_alife(specs) data  <- get_run_data(env) plot_run(data)  # Diploid run specs$ploidy <- 2L env2 <- run_alife(specs)  # BNN with epigenetics specs$brain_type  <- "bnn" specs$epigenetics <- TRUE env3 <- run_alife(specs) } # }

what: Simulate the evolution of scientific practice — run_bad_science
title: Simulate the evolution of scientific practice — run_bad_science
text: Implements agent-based model Smaldino & McElreath (2016). "Labs" (agent groups) heritable research_power (W; probability detecting real effect hypothesis test true) research_effort (e; methodological rigour, lowers per-test false-positive rate). tick, lab tests n_studies_per_tick hypotheses, priori true probability base_rate_true (b). Pub counts

title: Simulate the evolution of scientific practice — run_bad_science
code: run_bad_science(   n_labs = 200L,   n_ticks = 500L,   n_studies_per_tick = 5L,   replication_rate = 0,   replication_penalty = 5,   alpha_base = 0.5,   base_rate_true = 0.3,   research_power_init_mean = 0.3,   research_effort_init_mean = 0.8,   mutation_sd = 0.05,   seed = NULL )

title: Simulate the evolution of scientific practice — run_bad_science
text: n_labs Integer; number labs. Default 200. n_ticks Integer; number evolutionary ticks run. Default 500. n_studies_per_tick Integer; studies produced per lab per tick. Default 5. replication_rate Numeric [0, 1]; probability per tick lab attempts replicate one random neighbour's published findings. failed replication (original false positive) debits original lab's publication count replication_penalty. Default 0. replication_penalty Numeric \(\geq 0\); publication credits subtracted original lab replication attempt fails. Set 0 recover "replication without cost" behaviour. Default 5. alpha_base Numeric (0, 1]; per-test false-positive rate zero effort. FPR effort e alpha_base * (1 - e). Default 0.5, matching Smaldino & McElreath's \(\alpha_0\). base_rate_true Numeric (0, 1); prior probability tested hypothesis genuinely true. Default 0.3. Higher values mean larger share publications true positives, sharpens replication's ability discriminate low-effort labs; low values (< 0.15) swamp replication's signal almost every publication false positive regardless effort. research_power_init_mean Numeric; initial mean research power W. Default 0.3. research_effort_init_mean Numeric; initial mean research effort e. Default 0.8. mutation_sd Numeric; standard deviation Gaussian mutation applied traits reproduction. Default 0.05. seed Integer NULL; random seed reproducibility. Default NULL.

title: Simulate the evolution of scientific practice — run_bad_science
text: data frame one row per tick columns: t Tick number. mean_power Mean research_power across labs. mean_effort Mean research_effort across labs. mean_fpr Mean false-positive rate alpha across labs. total_publications Total publications produced tick. failed_replications Number failed replication attempts.

title: Simulate the evolution of scientific practice — run_bad_science
text: true positives  ~ Binomial(n_studies * b,       W) false positives ~ Binomial(n_studies * (1 - b), alpha(e)) alpha(e) = alpha_base * (1 - e). per-test FPR depends effort , W, matching Smaldino & McElreath's formulation. Labs publications reproduce higher rates. replication_rate > 0, lab probability per tick replicating random peer's random finding; failed replication (original false positive, happens probability alpha(1-b) / (Wb + alpha(1-b))) debits original lab's publication count replication_penalty. large enough penalty, replication culture selects low-effort labs slows reverses evolution high FPR.

title: Simulate the evolution of scientific practice — run_bad_science
text: Smaldino, P.E. & McElreath, R. (2016) natural selection bad science. Royal Society Open Science 3:160384. doi:10.1098/rsos.160384

title: Simulate the evolution of scientific practice — run_bad_science
code: result <- run_bad_science(n_ticks = 200L, seed = 1L) plot(result$t, result$mean_fpr, type = "l",      xlab = "Tick", ylab = "Mean false-positive rate",      main = "Evolution of bad science")

what: CMA-ES optimisation over simulation parameters — search_cmaes
title: CMA-ES optimisation over simulation parameters — search_cmaes
text: Optimises scalar objective function simulation parameter space using pure-R implementation Covariance Matrix Adaptation Evolution Strategy (CMA-ES; Hansen & Ostermeier 2001). Unlike MAP-Elites, CMA-ES finds single optimal parameter set adapting search distribution local curvature objective landscape.

title: CMA-ES optimisation over simulation parameters — search_cmaes
code: search_cmaes(   specs_base,   objective = "genetic_diversity",   params = c("grass_rate", "mutation_sd"),   n_iterations = 200L,   popsize = NULL,   sigma0 = 0.3,   n_cores = 1L,   verbose = TRUE )

title: CMA-ES optimisation over simulation parameters — search_cmaes
text: specs_base specs list default_specs(). objective Character function (search_map_elites()). params Character vector positive numeric parameter names optimise. Defaults c("grass_rate", "mutation_sd"). n_iterations Integer. Maximum CMA-ES generations (default 200L). popsize Integer NULL. CMA-ES population size lambda. NULL (default), uses standard formula max(8, 4 + floor(3 * log(n_params))). sigma0 Numeric. Initial step size log scale (default 0.3). n_cores Integer. Parallel cores candidate evaluation (default 1L). Uses parallel::makeCluster() PSOCK workers (one R session + Julia per worker) > 1. parallel::mclapply 0.5.6 path silently deadlocked JuliaConnectoR fork-safe — see dev/docs/parallelism-audit.md. verbose Logical (default TRUE).

title: CMA-ES optimisation over simulation parameters — search_cmaes
text: list : $specs Best specs encountered across generations. $score Best objective value encountered. $history Data frame one row per generation columns generation, evals, best_score, mean_score, sigma.

title: CMA-ES optimisation over simulation parameters — search_cmaes
text: Parameters optimised log scale positive constraints always satisfied. generation evaluates lambda candidate parameter sets, selects best mu = lambda/2, updates mean, step size, covariance matrix. external packages required.

title: CMA-ES optimisation over simulation parameters — search_cmaes
text: Hansen, N. & Ostermeier, . (2001) Completely derandomized self-adaptation evolution strategies. Evolutionary Computation 9(2):159-195. Hansen, N. (2006) CMA evolution strategy: comparing review. : Towards New Evolutionary Computation, Springer, pp 75-102.

title: CMA-ES optimisation over simulation parameters — search_cmaes
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ result <- search_cmaes(   default_specs(),   objective    = "genetic_diversity",   params       = c("grass_rate", "mutation_sd"),   n_iterations = 50L ) result$specs$grass_rate result$history   # one row per generation } # }

what: Finite-difference gradient ascent over simulation parameters — search_gradient
title: Finite-difference gradient ascent over simulation parameters — search_gradient
text: Optimises scalar objective respect named numeric simulation parameters using forward-difference gradient ascent. gradient coordinate estimated re-running run_alife() one parameter perturbed epsilon log scale, comparing resulting objective baseline run. Updates applied log scale positive parameters remain positive.

title: Finite-difference gradient ascent over simulation parameters — search_gradient
code: search_gradient(   specs_base,   params = c("grass_rate", "mutation_sd"),   objective = "genetic_diversity",   n_steps = 20L,   epsilon = 0.05,   learning_rate = 0.1,   n_cores = 1L,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Finite-difference gradient ascent over simulation parameters — search_gradient
text: specs_base specs list default_specs(). params Character vector numeric parameter names optimise. Defaults c("grass_rate", "mutation_sd"). objective Character function. character, name column get_run_data()$ticks maximise. function, must accept env list return numeric scalar. n_steps Integer. Number gradient ascent steps (default 20L). epsilon Numeric. Finite-difference step log scale (default 0.05). learning_rate Numeric. Log-scale step size parameter updates (default 0.1). n_cores Integer. Parallel cores finite-difference evaluation (default 1L). Finite-difference gradient needs length(params) + 1 evaluations per step, embarrassingly parallel. > 1, runs across parallel::makeCluster() PSOCK cluster (worker R session + Julia). Cluster created per call reused across steps. 0.5.6. verbose Logical. Print progress (default TRUE).

title: Finite-difference gradient ascent over simulation parameters — search_gradient
text: list components: $specs Best specs encountered across gradient steps. $score Best objective value encountered. $history Data frame one row per step columns step, score, one column per optimised parameter.

title: Finite-difference gradient ascent over simulation parameters — search_gradient
text: implementation intentionally backend-agnostic: treats run_alife() black box requires (n_steps + 1) * n_params simulation calls per run. true gradient--simulation approach using Zygote.jl automatic differentiation Julia backend, see deferred Phase 4b plan.

title: Finite-difference gradient ascent over simulation parameters — search_gradient
text: Spall, J.C. (1998) overview simultaneous perturbation method efficient optimization. Johns Hopkins APL Technical Digest 19(4):482-492. Innes, M. (2018) unroll adjoint: differentiating SSA-form programs. arXiv:1810.07951. (Zygote.jl – Phase 4b deferred.)

title: Finite-difference gradient ascent over simulation parameters — search_gradient
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ result <- search_gradient(   default_specs(),   params        = c("grass_rate", "mutation_sd"),   objective     = "genetic_diversity",   n_steps       = 20L,   epsilon       = 0.05,   learning_rate = 0.1 ) result$specs$grass_rate } # }

what: MAP-Elites quality-diversity search over simulation parameters — search_map_elites
title: MAP-Elites quality-diversity search over simulation parameters — search_map_elites
text: Finds diverse archive high-performing parameter combinations using MAP-Elites algorithm (Mouret & Clune 2015). Rather finding one optimal parameter set, MAP-Elites maintains grid niches behaviour space; cell holds best-performing specs behavioural region.

title: MAP-Elites quality-diversity search over simulation parameters — search_map_elites
code: search_map_elites(   specs_base,   archive_dims,   n_iterations = 1000L,   objective = "genetic_diversity",   mutation_params = NULL,   mutation_sd = 0.1,   verbose = TRUE,   checkpoint_path = NULL,   checkpoint_every = 100L )

title: MAP-Elites quality-diversity search over simulation parameters — search_map_elites
text: specs_base specs list default_specs(). Used starting point mutations. archive_dims named list whose names column names get_run_data()$ticks whose values numeric vectors specifying bin breakpoints dimension. Example: list(genetic_diversity = seq(0, 1, = 0.1), n_species = 1:10). n_iterations Integer. Number MAP-Elites iterations (default 1000L). objective Character function. character, name column get_run_data()$ticks maximise (e.g. "genetic_diversity"). function, must accept env list return numeric scalar. mutation_params Character vector parameter names (default_specs()) mutate. Defaults numeric parameters. mutation_sd Numeric. Standard deviation Gaussian perturbations log-transformed parameter values (default 0.1). verbose Logical. Print progress (default TRUE). checkpoint_path Optional file path. supplied, current archive + history + iteration index saved RDS file every checkpoint_every iterations (end). path passed subsequent call, search resumes saved iteration. Set NULL (default) disable checkpointing. Added 0.5.6. checkpoint_every Integer. often write checkpoint, iterations (default 100L). Ignored checkpoint_path NULL. Added 0.5.6.

title: MAP-Elites quality-diversity search over simulation parameters — search_map_elites
text: list components: $archive list lists, one per archive cell, containing: $specs, $score, $behavioural_descriptor (named numeric vector). $map ggplot2 heatmap archive scores (2D archive dims ). NULL higher-dimensional archives. $history Data frame one row per iteration: iteration, score, filled_cells.

title: MAP-Elites quality-diversity search over simulation parameters — search_map_elites
text: Initialise: fill archive cell sampling random specs near specs_base. n_iterations iterations: . Select random filled cell archive. b. Mutate specs (Gaussian perturbation numeric parameters). c. Run run_alife(new_specs). d. Compute behavioural descriptors get_run_data(env). e. new specs score better current occupant corresponding archive cell, replace . Return full archive.

title: MAP-Elites quality-diversity search over simulation parameters — search_map_elites
text: Mouret, J.-B. & Clune, J. (2015) Illuminating search spaces mapping elites. arXiv:1504.04909. Chatzilygeroudis, K., Cully, ., Vassiliades, V. & Mouret, J.-B. (2021) Quality-Diversity Optimization: novel branch stochastic optimization. arXiv:2012.04322.

title: MAP-Elites quality-diversity search over simulation parameters — search_map_elites
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ result <- search_map_elites(   specs_base   = default_specs(),   archive_dims = list(     genetic_diversity = seq(0, 1, by = 0.1),     n_species         = 1:10   ),   n_iterations = 500L,   objective    = "genetic_diversity" ) result$map   # ggplot2 heatmap } # }

what: Slow-generation specs for long-lived organism scenarios — slow_specs
title: Slow-generation specs for long-lived organism scenarios — slow_specs
text: Returns default_specs() calibrated long-lived organisms (elephant, whale, large primate). Generation time ~50 ticks; requires longer runs (10000+ ticks) meaningful evolution.

title: Slow-generation specs for long-lived organism scenarios — slow_specs
code: slow_specs()

title: Slow-generation specs for long-lived organism scenarios — slow_specs
text: specs list calibrated K-strategist organisms.

title: Stream a parameter-space sweep to disk, one row per run — stream_specs_to_csv
code: stream_specs_to_csv(   specs_list,   out_path,   summary_fn = NULL,   n_cores = 1L,   resume = TRUE,   flush_every = 1L )

title: Stream a parameter-space sweep to disk, one row per run — stream_specs_to_csv
text: specs_list List specs. out_path Path CSV. Created absent; appended present. First row always header. summary_fn Function (env, specs) -> named list one-row data.frame. return summary stats want save per run. Default: viability verdict + n_final + mean_energy_final genetic_diversity_final + run's random_seed params differ default_specs(). n_cores Integer. Parallel workers, batch_alife(). resume Logical. TRUE (default) out_path already exists, skip runs whose run_id existing CSV. Set FALSE overwrite. flush_every Integer. often flush CSV disk (rows). Default 1 (flush every row — safest; small /O cost).

title: Stream a parameter-space sweep to disk, one row per run — stream_specs_to_csv
text: path CSV (invisibly). Read back read.csv(out_path).

title: Stream a parameter-space sweep to disk, one row per run — stream_specs_to_csv
text: Memory: full env object run discarded summary row written, 1 M-run sweep accumulate 1 M copies progress / deaths RAM. Resumability: job dies run 800 000 1 000 000, re-running out_path picks last written row (matched run_id column).

title: Stream a parameter-space sweep to disk, one row per run — stream_specs_to_csv
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ specs_list <- sample_specs(fast_specs(), n = 10000L,   grass_rate  = list(0.05, 0.40),   mutation_sd = c(0.02, 0.05, 0.10)) stream_specs_to_csv(specs_list, "/tmp/sweep.csv", n_cores = 50L) tbl <- read.csv("/tmp/sweep.csv")   # even if 10k × 50 B = 500 KB } # }

what: Generate a SLURM array-job template for a parameter-space sweep — submit_sweep_slurm
title: Generate a SLURM array-job template for a parameter-space sweep — submit_sweep_slurm
text: Writes two files together let fan clade sweep across SLURM cluster: .rds full specs_list, shell script calls Rscript -e 'clade::stream_specs_to_csv(...)' subset specs per array task. run sweep sbatch <script>.sh — function talk SLURM.

title: Generate a SLURM array-job template for a parameter-space sweep — submit_sweep_slurm
code: submit_sweep_slurm(   specs_list,   out_path,   script_path,   rds_path,   n_array_tasks = min(100L, length(specs_list)),   n_cores_per_task = 4L,   time = "06:00:00",   mem = "8G",   summary_fn = NULL,   R_library_path = NULL,   extra_sbatch_lines = character(0) )

title: Generate a SLURM array-job template for a parameter-space sweep — submit_sweep_slurm
text: specs_list List specs (e.g. sample_specs()). out_path Character. Path cluster filesystem CSV appended . Must reachable every node. script_path Character. Local path .sh file written. rds_path Character. Local path .rds specs_list written. Cluster nodes need able read path; shared filesystem just put somewhere cluster can see. n_array_tasks Integer. Number SLURM array tasks. gets ceiling(length(specs_list) / n_array_tasks) specs. Default: min(100, length(specs_list)). n_cores_per_task Integer. n_cores passed stream_specs_to_csv() within array task. Default 4L. time Character. SLURM --time value (e.g. "06:00:00"). mem Character. SLURM --mem value (e.g. "8G"). summary_fn Optional summary function, stream_specs_to_csv(). NULL (default), default summary used cluster side. R_library_path Optional character. Added .libPaths() cluster node via .libPaths(c("<path>", .libPaths())) library(clade). Useful clade installed non-standard location cluster. extra_sbatch_lines Character vector extra #SBATCH directives (without leading #SBATCH) include script preamble.

title: Generate a SLURM array-job template for a parameter-space sweep — submit_sweep_slurm
text: path generated shell script (invisibly), message showing sbatch command invoke.

title: Generate a SLURM array-job template for a parameter-space sweep — submit_sweep_slurm
text: Per-task behaviour: reads SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID environment, selects corresponding slice specs_list, appends summary rows shared out_path CSV. stream_specs_to_csv() resume-safe, re-running array task idempotent — useful jobs get preempted.

title: Generate a SLURM array-job template for a parameter-space sweep — submit_sweep_slurm
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ specs_list <- sample_specs(fast_specs(), n = 100000L,                            grass_rate  = list(0.05, 0.45),                            mutation_sd = c(0.05, 0.1, 0.2)) submit_sweep_slurm(   specs_list,   out_path     = "/shared/sweeps/big_sweep.csv",   script_path  = "/shared/sweeps/submit.sh",   rds_path     = "/shared/sweeps/specs.rds",   n_array_tasks = 200L,   n_cores_per_task = 8L,   time         = "12:00:00",   mem          = "16G") # then: sbatch /shared/sweeps/submit.sh } # }

what: Spec preset for the Trivers 1971 reciprocal-altruism reproduction — trivers_reciprocity_specs
title: Spec preset for the Trivers 1971 reciprocal-altruism reproduction — trivers_reciprocity_specs
text: Returns specs list configured demonstrate conditions Trivers (1971) identified evolution conditional cooperation: long lifespan, low dispersal, partner recognition, cheater discrimination. See vignette paper-trivers1971.Rmd full discussion dispersal sweep maps cooperation-vs-defection regime boundary.

title: Spec preset for the Trivers 1971 reciprocal-altruism reproduction — trivers_reciprocity_specs
code: trivers_reciprocity_specs()

title: Spec preset for the Trivers 1971 reciprocal-altruism reproduction — trivers_reciprocity_specs
text: specs list ready run_alife().

title: Spec preset for the Trivers 1971 reciprocal-altruism reproduction — trivers_reciprocity_specs
text: Key parameter choices vs default_specs(): reciprocal_altruism = TRUE Enable module. max_age = 500L Long lifespan (Trivers condition 1) — many opportunities repeat encounters. dispersal_evolution = FALSE Low dispersal (Trivers condition 2) — partners stay nearby, re-encounter rate high. grid_rows/grid_cols = 30L Standard density. reciprocity radius defaults 1 (Moore neighborhood). n_agents_init = 200L Higher density (~22%) → frequent adjacency encounters. max_agents = 800L Room population grow mutual cooperation. max_ticks = 2000L Long enough selection three reciprocity traits act. ploidy = 1L Haploid asexual; cleaner trait dynamics. Trivers-specific parameters (cost, benefit ratio, interaction rate, partner memory size, encounter radius, trait init means/SDs) inherit defaults default_specs(). run dispersal-rate sweep demonstrates regime boundary:
code: for (rate in c(0, 0.1, 0.3, 0.5)) {   s <- trivers_reciprocity_specs()   s$dispersal_evolution    <- TRUE   s$dispersal_init_mean    <- rate   s$dispersal_mutation_sd  <- 0   # lock dispersal at this rate   env <- run_alife(s)   # ... compute mean cooperation rate from final agent traits }

what: Ultra-realistic specs for finite-size-sensitive audits — ultra_realistic_specs
title: Ultra-realistic specs for finite-size-sensitive audits — ultra_realistic_specs
text: Returns realistic_specs() scaled scenarios whose theoretical signal dominated finite-population corrections — notably Red Queen (Otto & Michalakis 1998: advantage scales ~μN) Hamilton 1971 selfish herd (risk dilution scales ~1/√N). realistic_specs() equilibrium N ≈ 120, signals 5–10× analytical limits.

title: Ultra-realistic specs for finite-size-sensitive audits — ultra_realistic_specs
code: ultra_realistic_specs()

title: Ultra-realistic specs for finite-size-sensitive audits — ultra_realistic_specs
text: specs list ecological-theory scale (N ≈ 800–1500 equilibrium, 80 generations).

title: Ultra-realistic specs for finite-size-sensitive audits — ultra_realistic_specs
text: Preserves realistic_specs() settings except: grid_rows, grid_cols 120L × 120L (16× default area, 4× realistic). n_agents_init 800L. Seeded expected equilibrium transient overshoot hit max_agents. max_agents 5000L. Supports larger carrying capacity. max_ticks 2500L. ~80 generations max_age = 30. Longer runs destabilise BNN kernel. predator_max_agents 400L. Typical wall time: 3–6 minutes per run (15M agent-ticks); 8 seeds parallel 16 PSOCK workers finish one coffee break. Fits comfortably 200-core / 300-GB machine budget.

what: Viability report for an evolutionary-audit run — viability_report
title: Viability report for an evolutionary-audit run — viability_report
text: Checks whether run_alife() result viable enough support claims evolved trait values. Population crashes (agents dying faster reproduce) silently corrupt trait-mean audits -weighting lucky survivors. function quantifies crash risk via three metrics returns tidy report together verdict {"viable", "weak", "crashed"}.

title: Viability report for an evolutionary-audit run — viability_report
code: viability_report(   run_data,   n_agents_init = NULL,   crashed_frac = 0.2,   weak_frac = 0.5,   min_n = 20L )

title: Viability report for an evolutionary-audit run — viability_report
text: run_data list get_run_data() — either single $ticks data frame full get_run_data() output. n_agents_init Integer. initial agent count used seed run. Required run_data carry spec. Pass NULL (default) use first-tick n_agents (approximates init-mean first wave births, usually close enough). crashed_frac Numeric (0, 1). run declared "crashed" n_final < crashed_frac * n_agents_init. Default 0.2 (final population less 20% init). weak_frac Numeric (0, 1). run declared "weak" (viable low confidence) n_final < weak_frac * n_agents_init. Default 0.5. min_n Integer. Absolute minimum n_final run "crashed" regardless crashed_frac. Default 20 (fewer 20 agents, trait mean dominated handful individuals). Set 0 disable floor.

title: Viability report for an evolutionary-audit run — viability_report
text: list : verdict One "viable", "weak", "crashed". n_init First-tick n_agents used reference. n_final Last-tick n_agents. n_min Minimum n_agents across whole run. frac_final n_final / n_init. frac_min n_min / n_init. tick_of_min First tick n_min reached. message one-line diagnostic suitable logging.

title: Viability report for an evolutionary-audit run — viability_report
text: Motivation: 2026-04-17 fast_specs re-audit s-plasticity s-dispersal-ifd, direction flips 5-seed multi-seed results traced seasonal runs n_final < 20 stable runs maintained healthy populations. trait-mean average 0-5 surviving agents dominated specific crash trajectory rather evolutionary signal. utility codifies "check n_final trusting trait-mean effects" rule reusable check.

title: Viability report for an evolutionary-audit run — viability_report
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ env  <- run_alife(fast_specs()) vr   <- viability_report(get_run_data(env)) print(vr) # Guard audit claims on viability: if (vr$verdict == "crashed") {   warning("crash-driven result; trait means are unreliable") } } # }

what: Spec preset for the Wolf 2008 responsive-personalities reproduction — wolf2008_responsiveness_specs
title: Spec preset for the Wolf 2008 responsive-personalities reproduction — wolf2008_responsiveness_specs
text: Returns specs list configured test negative-frequency-dependent- selection mechanism Wolf, van Doorn & Weissing (2008) PNAS 105:15825-15830, clade's spatially-explicit framework. See vignette paper-wolf2008.Rmd full reproduction context.

title: Spec preset for the Wolf 2008 responsive-personalities reproduction — wolf2008_responsiveness_specs
code: wolf2008_responsiveness_specs()

title: Spec preset for the Wolf 2008 responsive-personalities reproduction — wolf2008_responsiveness_specs
text: specs list ready run_alife().

title: Spec preset for the Wolf 2008 responsive-personalities reproduction — wolf2008_responsiveness_specs
text: Key parameter choices vs default_specs(): responsive_personalities = TRUE Enable module. grid_rows/grid_cols = 30L Standard grid; rich-cell competition emerges naturally clade's grass economy. n_agents_init = 200L Density 22% — high enough responsiveness's frequency-dependent cost actually bites. max_agents = 800L Room population growth; regulator grass + handling time, max_agents. max_ticks = 3000L ~15 generations default max_age = 200. Long enough responsiveness trait evolve toward equilibrium frequency. ploidy = 1L Haploid asexual; cleaner trait dynamics. responsiveness_cost = 0.1 Calibrated default_specs() value 0.4 populations collapse trait evolution. frequency-dependent benefit mechanism preserved either way. Wolf 2008-specific parameters listed (responsiveness_init_mean, mutation SD) inherit default_specs(). probe density-dependent benefit (Wolf's headline mechanism):
code: for (n_init in c(50L, 100L, 200L, 400L)) {   s <- wolf2008_responsiveness_specs()   s$n_agents_init <- n_init   env <- run_alife(s)   # ... compute mean responsiveness at end and plot vs n_init }

what: Spec preset for the Wolf 2007 personality reproduction — wolf_personality_specs
title: Spec preset for the Wolf 2007 personality reproduction — wolf_personality_specs
text: Returns specs list configured reproduce boldness-aggressiveness syndrome Wolf, van Doorn, Leimar & Weissing (2007) Nature 447:581-584, implemented clade's spatially-explicit framework. See vignette paper-wolf2007.Rmd full reproduction context discussion spatially-explicit interpretation (vs Wolf's mean-field model).

title: Spec preset for the Wolf 2007 personality reproduction — wolf_personality_specs
code: wolf_personality_specs()

title: Spec preset for the Wolf 2007 personality reproduction — wolf_personality_specs
text: specs list ready run_alife().

title: Spec preset for the Wolf 2007 personality reproduction — wolf_personality_specs
text: Key parameter choices vs default_specs(): personality_syndrome = TRUE Enable module. min_repro_energy = 1e9 Disable clade's standard energy-triggered reproduction Wolf age-windowed reproduction fires. min_repro_age = 0L Defer reproduction control personality module's age windows. max_age = 999L Defer death control personality module (year-2 reproduction kills parent). grid_rows/grid_cols = 30L Standard density (Wolf used non-spatial population; clade needs grid large enough one-per-cell + neighborhood encounters). n_agents_init = 60L Initial density ~7%. max_agents = 500L Standard cap. max_ticks = 2000L 20 generations default wolf_year2_repro_age = 100. Wolf used 50,000 generations paper figures — exact reproduction, scale much higher. ploidy = 1L Haploid asexual genetics, matching Wolf's basic model (Methods §"Basic model"). diploid quantitative-genetics extension (Wolf's Fig 4) can enabled setting ploidy = 2L. Wolf-specific parameters (β, f_high, f_low, V, δ, b, γ, year ages, per-tick game frequencies) inherit defaults default_specs(). Override individual fields calling function:
code: s <- wolf_personality_specs() s$personality_hawkdove_radius <- 2L   # widen pairing neighborhood s$max_ticks <- 5000L                   # longer evolution env <- run_alife(s)

title: clade 0.7.1 (2026-05-13) — post-0.7.0 audit cleanup + CI re-enable

Found resolved 12 unwired -claimed entries: Brain-architecture -claim corrected: DESCRIPTION, README, R/clade-package.R said clade “six brain architectures”. reality four implemented (BNN, ANN, CTRNN, GRN) plus random baseline; transformer synthesis reserved names error kernel. three user-facing surfaces now match. world_evolution module deleted: three spec fields claiming enable feature whose Julia module file never existed (include line Clade.jl commented ). Removed fields. Six unwired spec fields deleted (Tier-3 cleanup): wall_density, wall_clusters, life_history_evolution, parental_investment_init_mean, repro_senescence. Bonus fix: three new 0.7.0 preset functions (wolf_personality_specs, trivers_reciprocity_specs, wolf2008_responsiveness_specs) @export tags missing NAMESPACE. Users released 0.7.0 tarball got “find function”. devtools::document() propagation added.

Adding spec field R without Julia consumer now fails test, generalising 0.6.4 mate_choice_mode incident class--bug guard.

Fixed DESCRIPTION (0.6.5 → 0.7.0), R/clade-package.R citation (0.5.6 → 0.7.0), inst/CITATION (0.3.0 → 0.7.0), README.md citation block + fidelity-audit badge (32/32 → 35/35 reflecting 0.7.0 paper reproductions), README modules table flag-name corrections (mate_choice → mate_choice_mode, predators → n_predators_init > 0, signal_mating → signal_dims > 0), README rows three 0.7.0 modules (personality_syndrome, reciprocal_altruism, responsive_personalities). Julia first-call compile time now consistently reported 60-90 s four places written 3-10, 10-90, 60-90 s. julia_is_ready() documentation corrected — trigger compilation; first run_alife() call .

what: Two MATLAB-ancestor regressions fixed in the kernel
title: clade 0.7.0 (2026-05-05) — kernel discipline + three personality/cooperation papers
text: clade descends Bulitko-group MATLAB code via alifeR R port. Cross-codebase audit (dev/docs/consolidation-audit.md) found two systematic biases chain ports lost: Random-asynchronous agent scheduling (Grimm & Railsback 2005; MATLAB alife.m:324). clade iterating env.agents array order every tick — earlier-array agents systematically moved/ate/ mated first. Fixed 9 sites across 8 files; new spec field random_tick_order (default TRUE) controls. FALSE, legacy behaviour recoverable reproducibility. One-per-cell movement (MATLAB takeAction.m:79). clade allowed silent agent stacking single cell; movement now rejects target cell occupied. New cell_to_agents Matrix Environment true multi-occupancy lookup sites need . Spec field max_agents_per_cell (default 1) controls. Bonus: offspring placement also enforced one-per-cell. Previously newborns landed parent’s cell, immediately stacking. Fixed _place_offspring scan Moore neighbourhood pick free cell; none, offspring (matching MATLAB). viability_report() learned skip absolute min_n check flat-population scenarios (n_init >= min_n); 2 stale test assertions unrelated kernel fixes corrected time.

title: clade 0.7.0 (2026-05-05) — kernel discipline + three personality/cooperation papers
text: three spatially-explicit clade interpretations rather faithful re-implementations analytic mean-field models — clade’s value-add precisely encounters happen neighbouring agents resources regulated grid, abstract patches. vignette honest interpretation strengthens weakens original prediction.

what: Wolf et al. 2007 Nature — personality syndrome
title: clade 0.7.0 (2026-05-05) — kernel discipline + three personality/cooperation papers
text: vignette("paper-wolf2007"). Hawk-dove pairs Moore neighbourhood; anti-predator games anchored real predator positions; year-1/year-2 reproduction fixed agent ages life-history trade-g(x) = (1-x)^β. Nine traits added genome (exploration, boldness, aggressiveness plus six gating fields). title: clade 0.6.0 (2026-04-19) — research workflow + paper reproductions
text: hypothesis_sweep(base_specs, conditions, seeds, metrics, n_cores) — wrap sweep-test-report pattern used across fidelity audits single researcher-facing helper. Crosses list named conditions seeds, dispatches via batch_alife(), computes user-supplied metric functions run’s tick log, returns tidy hypothesis_sweep S3 object. hypothesis_report(sweep, contrasts, metric) — Welch two-sample t-statistics named pairwise contrasts. Uses fidelity-audit 2σ screening convention (|t| ≥ 2 → PASS, 1.5 ≤ |t| < 2 → marginal, else null). Print methods render compact tables. Together turn 5-line sweep |> report |> interpret workflow default researcher idiom. See vignette("paper-kokko-brooks-2003") canonical example.

title: clade 0.6.0 (2026-04-19) — research workflow + paper reproductions
text: Five worked examples taking published behavioural-ecology paper, translating quantitative prediction clade experiment, reporting reproduces versus doesn’t. vignette demonstrates 3-stage workflow (grid-search → multi-seed validate → diagnose) covers different theoretical domain: paper-kokko-brooks-2003 — “Sexy die ?”. Tests whether costly sexual signals hurt populations environmental stress. clade contradicts K&B’s interaction direction robustly across 5 × 4 grid — mechanistic-level mismatch linear per-tick cost (clade) stress-multiplicative cost (K&B’s theoretical framework). paper-griesser-2023 — “Parental provisioning drives brain size birds” (PNAS). Grid search finds best-signal regime (cost_scale=1.5); 8-seed validation gives Spearman = +0.25 predicted direction sub-2σ. Direction-correct -threshold — honest outcome many empirical -silico verifications. paper-dieckmann-doebeli-1999 — “origin species sympatric speciation” (Nature). Clean ✅ decisive magnitude (t = +3.32 PASS, Spearman = −0.57). speciation kernel module matches paper’s mechanism, reproductions decisive. paper-reale-2010 — pace--life syndromes. Core lifespan-vs-metabolic-rate prediction reproduces Spearman = −0.98, t = −358 — one cleanest matches suite. Secondary traits (per-tick births, energy) clade-specific nuances surfaced multi-metric hypothesis_sweep(). paper-emlen-1982 — ecological constraints helping. Raw helping counts invert Emlen’s prediction; per-capita rate recovers direction. Reinforces s-kin invasion-dynamics honest-null session’s sweet-spot sweeps: helper_tendency evolve ecological constraint clade’s current kernel.

Distribution across 32 auditable scenarios plus 2 ⚪ N/: 10 ✅ citation + fidelity clean 14 ⚠️ direction-correct documented caveats (commonly, clade reproduces corollary paper’s claim rather claim ) 5 🟠 direction-correct transient contradicted evolving-ABM conditions cited theory assumes fixed-strategy predators, unlimited food, similar 0 ❌ outright retractions unsupported claims Three citation-precision corrections shipped -session (Hauert 2006 / Killingback 1999 s-cooperation’s continuous-strategy mechanism; Hamilton, Axelrod & Tanese 1990 alongside Hamilton 1980 s-mating-systems’ discrete-allele Red Queen; Kermack & McKendrick 1927 s-disease’s missing SIR citation).

title: clade 0.6.0 (2026-04-19) — research workflow + paper reproductions
text: Three sweeps demonstrating find parameter regime canonical theoretical prediction expresses clade: s-kin invasion-dynamics sweep — tested whether heritable helper_tendency invades rare Hamilton-satisfying cost regimes. Result: honest null — demographic consequence kin altruism stands (Spearman = 0.97 across rB/C), allele-invasion dynamics don’t reproduce current cooperative_breeding plumbing. Kernel needs kin-weighted fitness accounting. s-niche heritable-feedback sweep — tested Odling-Smee et al. 2003’s heritable-niche-construction claim. Clean ✅ shelter_occupancy_bonus > 0: Spearman(bonus, final_n) = +0.863, t = +6–8 PASS across three bonus levels. Notable finding: niche construction alone (bonus = 0) net cost; heritable feedback flips large benefit, exactly Odling-Smee’s framework predicts. s-parental-care variance-buffering sweep — tested Clutton-Brock 1991’s buffering prediction tighter resource scarcity. Conditional ✅ grass_rate = 0.08, care_cost_per_tick = 3.0: variance drops 58% (t = −2.61). Honest caveat: care halves equilibrium size, CV rises even absolute variance falls. sweep methodology now template finding “sweet spots” canonical prediction — conditions theory reproduces exactly versus conditions doesn’t. See matching fidelity reports dev/audit/fidelity/.

text: 80-run strength sweep (baseline + 4 strengths × 16 seeds) realistic_specs. mean-population test sub-2σ every strength (consistent 8-seed realistic_specs direction-finding) — signal lives extinction rate: group_defense_strength = 3.0, odds crashing 4.73× higher without group defense (75% → 38% crash rate). Fisher one-sided p = 0.037 crosses 2σ / p < 0.05 threshold. Right metric, right theory: Hamilton 1971 selfish herd per-capita predation risk survival. Aggregated population scale, manifests extinction-rate reduction, equilibrium-population increase. previous population-mean framing averaged survivors missed risk-dilution effect. Strength-dependence biologically sensible: weak defense (strength = 0.5) doesn’t help, moderate (1.0) marginal (p = 0.074), strong (3.0) decisive. what: Lotka-Volterra in clade — an honest null
title: clade 0.5.12 (2026-04-18, LV discovery experiment)
text: Added discovery experiment predator_prey_lv.R tests whether textbook Lotka-Volterra cycles can coaxed clade’s predator kernel. Four conditions × 5 seeds × 2000 ticks realistic_specs(): evolving (default): predator_mutation_sd = 0.1. frozen: predator_mutation_sd = 0 (new brain variance). longlived: predator_max_age = 300 (10× prey lifespan, slow-pace--life; lynx > hare). Ecologically natural “fixed per-capita attack” approximation via slow evolution. fast_turnover: short-lived (max_age=20) + easy reproduction (min_repro_energy=60) + frozen brains — classical LV-style demographic turnover. Result: condition produces textbook LV. evolving / frozen / longlived: pred_var = 0 t=500 — predator population flatlines ~30 regardless prey dynamics. fast_turnover: predators explode 40 10,000 (cap) prey crash ~5. Phase plot one-way trajectory, closed LV loop. Boom-bust runaway, cycling. . LV requires dP/dt ∝ prey × predator — predator births proportional prey density. clade’s predator reproduction energy-threshold-gated (reproduce energy ≥ X), produces saturation (hard threshold) runaway (easy threshold) never density-matched cycling. lynx-hare cycles nature exist mammalian reproduction density-dependent via gestation/provisioning constraints. clade’s predator module like bacterial predator mammalian carnivore. Kernel extension 0.6+ LV cycles become required audit signal. Companion figure: vignettes/figures/showcase_14b_predators_lv_comparison.png. Vignette updated new discovery-experiment section.

One demotion: s-stress-hypermutation ✅ → 🟠: real diploid sex, baseline genetic diversity (0.263) already equals hypermutation produces (0.263). Δ = +0.000 across 4 seeds grass_rate = 0.06. Rosenberg 2001 / Foster 2007’s “hypermutation raises diversity” prediction reproduce tested parameters. Primary claims hold 11: s-pop-genetics (h² proxy = 0.988): ✅ holds s-speciation (P1+P2+P3 PASS, ρ(iso, n_species) = −0.97): ✅ s-kin (P1 PASS Δ=+21.3, P3 PASS; P2 slightly weakened): ✅ s-cooperation (ρ(mult, pop) = 1.00): ✅ s-brain-size (Δdelta = +1.112 best regime): ✅ s-body-size (Cope drift +0.042 ± 0.011; P2 flat documented): ✅ s-parental-investment (ρ(fi, juveniles) = −1.00 per Trivers): ✅ s-clutch-size (Lack positive grass 0.05→0.20; inverts resource-saturation 0.30+ due max_agents cap): ✅ reframed s-life-history (3/3 PASS: semelparous vs iteroparous): ✅ s-pace--life (ρ(metabolic_rate, age) = −1.00): ✅ s-parental-care (P1+P2 PASS): ✅ See dev/audit/fidelity/post_0510_summary.md. Final ledger: 26 ✅ / 6 🟠 / 0 🔴 32 auditable scenarios (81% ✅, confirmed end--end real-diploid-sex kernel).

what: Kernel bug: silent haploid conversion under mate-finding failure
title: clade 0.5.9 (2026-04-18, late evening)
text: re-auditing s-plasticity s-baldwin correct metric (mean_prior_sigma, neutral mean_plasticity trait), found mean_prior_sigma pegged exactly bnn_sigma_init = 0.5 across seeds / envs / mutation rates, regardless selection. Root cause inst/julia/src/genome.jl:make_offspring_genome: diploid agent find mate (Allee-failure realistic grid densities), fallback path sets pat_w = Float32[]. resulting offspring empty paternal-weights vector, make_bnn_brain takes is_haploid branch assigns sigma = fill(bnn_sigma_init, n). Within generations entire diploid population silently converts sigma-pegged “effectively haploid” agents, Baldwin canalization signal (heterozygosity purging stable envs) observed. Fix (opt-): new spec self_fertilization_fallback (default FALSE backward compatibility). TRUE, fallback path instead calls meiosis(parent1, ...) second time — offspring stays diploid two gametes parent (self-fertilization). selfing enabled, mean_prior_sigma drops pegged 0.5 real heterozygosity-derived ~0.076 evolves .

what: Why this didn’t promote plasticity/Baldwin to ✅
title: clade 0.5.9 (2026-04-18, late evening)

Full promotion require one : - Broader mate search (5×5 7×7 neighbourhood instead Moore 8) Allee-failure becomes rare full-diploid evolution dominates. Ecologically reasonable. - Outcrossed fallback (random cross-grid sperm donor instead parent1) -mate offspring isn’t inbred. kernel changes outside 0.5.9 scope.

what: BNN sigma decoupling + ultra_realistic_specs audit cycle
title: clade 0.5.8 (2026-04-18, evening)
text: Followed morning’s realistic_specs() work two priorities 🟠-analysis reflection: (1) activate existing BNN action-noise / sigma-lr decoupling plasticity/Baldwin/RL audits, (2) add bigger preset finite-size-sensitive scenarios. Promotion: s-rl 🟠 → ✅ 16 seeds × realistic_specs bnn_action_noise_scale = 0.7, bnn_sample_freq = 5, rl_update_freq = 5, learning_rate_init_mean = 0.005: Δn_agents(actor_critic − none) = +10.9 ± 4.9 t = +2.20 (17% larger equilibrium population). Williams 1992 REINFORCE works agent can actually exploit learned posterior mean; legacy sigma-coupled action noise re-randomising policy every tick cancelling learning signal. New preset: ultra_realistic_specs() — 120×120 grid, 500 init, 5000 max, 2500 ticks, 400-agent equilibrium. Designed Red-Queen-type scenarios whose theoretical signal scales N. Null findings (honestly documented, verdict change): s-plasticity, s-baldwin: BNN sigma decoupling bnn_sigma_source = "trait" non-viable realistic scale (0–2 seeds per cell survive). viable "heterozygosity" mode plasticity trait neutral marker, Δ = 0. Genuine kernel limitation — decoupling infrastructure exists trait-mode sigma source needs stability work. s-mating-systems: 32 seeds × ultra_realistic_specs gives Δn_sex−asex = +2.4 t = +0.41 (smaller 16-seed ultra result +7.6 — seed noise). Otto & Michalakis 1998’s ~μN finite-size scaling manifest clade’s discrete-allele parasite kernel. s-group-defense ultra scale: Δ = +0.66 t = +0.08 — signal vanishes. Correct finite-size interpretation selfish-herd risk dilution (∝ 1/√N) means larger herds need defense less, . Vignette reframe (P3): s-predation-neural — vignette’s “Expected output” section rewritten split two historical claims: () predation reduces prey population (Williams 1966 demographic, ✅ t = −3.64), (b) predation maintains genetic diversity via directional selection (retracted, t = −0.90 clade’s mutation-bounded brain-weight regime). Returns empty-state plot column absent -zero. breaking change. plot_kin_network() — remains placeholder (igraph clade dependency). Docstring rewritten flag loudly anyone autocompleting function sees “yet implemented” trying use . search_gradient(n_cores) — previously documented “Reserved future parallel finite-difference evaluation; currently unused”. Now actually uses n_cores via single PSOCK cluster reused across gradient steps. Finite-difference embarrassingly parallel: length(params) + 1 independent evaluations per step. Plus CLAUDE.md integration four karpathy principles top file, clade-specific application note. Substantial multi-PR day covering timescale presets, 🟠-sort work, new audit-methodology utility, working parallel scenario-search toolkit, three kernel bug fixes, head--head brain-type benchmark. 22+ PRs merged main.

title: clade 0.5.6 (2026-04-17)
text: story: existing batch_alife(n_cores = N) path silently deadlocked (forked R workers blocked one Julia socket — fork-unsafe RPC). Fix: swap parallel::mclapply parallel::makeCluster("PSOCK"), worker separate R process Julia session. fix applied search_cmaes. n_cores > 1 now actually parallelises. New exports built fixed parallel path: grid_specs(base, ...) — factorial expansion base spec. sample_specs(base, n, ...) — random draws three distribution forms (vector → sample replacement; list 2 → uniform; function → user-supplied). summarize_batch(results, specs_list) — one-row-per-run tidy data frame parameters, default metrics, viability verdict. stream_specs_to_csv(specs_list, out_path) — memory-efficient streaming writer 10k–1M-scenario sweeps. resume = TRUE (default) skips run_ids already output CSV, killed overnight jobs restart cleanly. submit_sweep_slurm() — writes SLURM array-job template dispatches chunks specs_list across cluster nodes, task calling stream_specs_to_csv() shared CSV. search_map_elites(checkpoint_path, checkpoint_every) — checkpoint/resume long MAP-Elites runs. New dedicated vignette: parameter-space-search.Rmd (placed parameter-reference sidebar) walks full workflow small grids million-scenario cluster sweeps. Full deadlock post-mortem: dev/docs/parallelism-audit.md.

title: clade 0.5.6 (2026-04-17)
text: body_size foraging death spiral: apply_body_size! charged small agents (bs < 1) foraging correction bounded 40% current energy — low-grass cells correction exceeded eat_gain, making eating net-negative. Small agents drifted bs ≈ 0.5 starved. Fix: scale eat_gain * bite * body_size source tick.jl; drop post-hoc correction. realistic mutation_sd (≤ 0.02) scenario now viable fast_specs. RNG-order contamination BNN sampler: randn(length(mu)) Thompson sampling used Julia’s global RNG. Consecutive run_alife() calls one Julia session shared state figures depended call order even random_seed set. Fix: per-run RNG cache populated env.rng top tick_agents!. Verified deterministic across call order. search_cmaes parallel path: PSOCK fix batch_alife. Single cluster reused across generations amortise Julia compile cost.

title: clade 0.5.6 (2026-04-17)
text: bnn_action_noise_scale (0.5.5, now documented): decouples BNN sigma action sampling. scale = 0, actions deterministic mu; sigma affects learning/cost. action_exploration_epsilon (0.5.6): epsilon-greedy exploration orthogonal BNN sigma. epsilon > 0, probability tick picks uniformly random action. Intended exploration channel sigma fully decoupled. bnn_sigma_lr_scale, bnn_sigma_lr_ref (0.5.6): within-life effective learning rate scales mean(sigma)/sigma_ref. Canalised agents learn slowly; plastic agents learn fast. Puts cost canalisation learning speed, noise. Combined, three levers give 3-axis decoupling sigma channel. None individually promoted Baldwin ✅ — baseline sigma dynamics clade don’t canalise fast_specs settings, levers never engage. Filed clearest next step dev/audit/fidelity/ORANGE_OVERVIEW.md.

what: Audit: s-mating-systems 0.5.1 “first sex > asex” claim retracted
title: clade 0.5.3 (2026-04-16)
text: Applied 0.5.2 body-size precedent (16-seed sweep 2×SE hypothesis test) Red Queen scenario. Across 448 audit runs covering 19 distinct parameter regimes: 16-seed replication 0.5.1 default parasite_discrete parameters: Δn (sex − asex) = −0.49 ± 1.54 → flat within 2×SE. 0.5.1 “+1.1 first sex wins” finding 3-seed noise. Regime search n_loci × pressure × exponent × mutation (16 cells, 8 seeds ): 16 regimes show direction favour sex average; NONE crosses 2×SE. Top-3 verification 16 seeds: 8-seed apparent signals (+2.3 +2.8) collapse flat Δn ∈ {−1.07, +0.42, −0.45}. canonical discrete-allele Red Queen mechanism implemented correctly (direction consistently non-negative average), clade’s baseline cost sex higher parasite selection pressure can offset every tested regime. Hamilton (1980) noted sex’s two-fold cost tall order parasites; finding consistent caveat. Verdict: s-mating-systems stays 🟠 direction correct “first sex wins” claim retracted. Pushing ✅ deferred scenario-design question (minimise clade’s baseline cost sex via population size, mate-finding dynamics, longer runs). kernel changes.

title: clade 0.5.1 (2026-04-16)
text: 0.5.0’s continuous-trait parasite module produce Hamilton’s canonical Red Queen — sex offspring midpoints parents cluster near parasite optimum, sex exposed. 0.5.1 adds discrete-allele variant reproduces textbook effect. New Agent field parasite_haplotype::Vector{Int32} (heritable binary haplotype length n_parasite_loci). Mendelian inheritance free recombination: diploid offspring inherit locus independently either parent, producing genuinely novel haplotype combinations. Haploid clone + per-locus mutation. Hamming-distance matching parasite module: per-host penalty = pressure × ((n_loci − hamming) / n_loci)^exponent. Hosts exactly matching parasite haplotype pay full pressure; mismatched hosts escape cleanly. New specs: n_parasite_loci (default 0L), parasite_match_mode ("auto" / "continuous" / "discrete"), parasite_mutation_rate (0.01), parasite_discrete_exponent (4.0).

text: Tuning grid found regimes Δn +7.7 n_loci = 16, pressure = 2.0, exponent = 6, mutation = 0.02. audit-default parameters (3 seeds × 500 ticks): Δn = +1.1 parasite_discrete condition. P2 PASS (canonical Red Queen direction). P3 PASS (continuous-trait parasites disfavour sex, predicted 0.5.0). s-mating-systems stays 🟠 (magnitude modest) direction now matches Hamilton 1980.

title: clade 0.5.0 (2026-04-16)
text: New inst/julia/src/modules/coevolving_parasite.jl. Collective parasite “population” represented single virulence-genotype vector tracks host signal centroid lag. Per-host Gaussian-falloff energy penalty based Euclidean distance parasite optimum. New specs: coevolving_parasites (default FALSE), parasite_virulence_rate (0.1), parasite_pressure (0.5), parasite_distance_scale (1.0). Opt-; -op unless signal_dims > 0.

title: clade 0.5.0 (2026-04-16)
text: Continuous-trait parasites produce Hamilton’s canonical Red Queen. continuous matching, sex offspring (midpoint parents) sit closer population centroid asex clones, sex exposed. Audit verdict: Δn = −2.5 parasite_continuous. mechanism nevertheless models real biological phenomenon — parasites selecting genetic convergence — scaffolding set 0.5.1 implement Hamilton’s discrete-allele variant.

title: clade 0.4.4 (2026-04-16)
text: Dedicated signal_memory::Vector{Float32} field Agent replaces 0.4.0 Tier 4’s overloading preference (meant prey mate-choice). Cleaner semantics; mechanism. Delta-rule Rescorla-Wagner update apply_predator_toxin!. predator learns linear model predicts toxicity signal vector: Widrow-Hoff rule, standard supervised linear regression. Symmetric (reinforcement toxic prey, extinction non-toxic prey) → enables Batesian breakdown palatable mimics outnumber toxic models. Avoidance uses predicted toxicity: avoid dot(memory, signal) >= avoid_threshold. Aposematic pleiotropy — new spec signal_toxicity_coupling ∈ [0, 1]. > 0, agent’s signal[1] pulled toward toxicity tick — honest-signal mechanism theory requires.
code: memory += lr × (tox − dot(memory, signal)) × signal

title: clade 0.4.4 (2026-04-16)
text: Counter-summation bug dev/audit/fidelity/mimicry.R: tail(d$n_avoided_attacks, 1L) replaced sum(...) — per-tick counters reset tick, tail spuriously 0. s-mimicry P3 (learning fires) FAIL → PASS (12–28 avoidances / 600 ticks). P4 (dose-response) FAIL → PASS (Spearman ρ = +0.40). P5 (pleiotropy direction, new) PASS (ρ = +1.0). Magnitudes still parameter-sensitive; s-mimicry stays 🟠 substantially richer kernel semantics.

title: clade 0.4.3 (2026-04-16)
text: Two opt-features implementing biological mechanisms Aiello & Wheeler (1995) Isler & van Schaik (2009) invoke parental-provisioning scenarios, letting s-brain-size reach ✅ default brain_energy_base without scenario-specific overrides. Neonatal foraging deficit (neonatal_foraging_deficit, neonatal_deficit_duration R/config.R). first neonatal_deficit_duration ticks life, effective max_bite scaled (1 - deficit). Parental care bypasses via existing feeding_rate channel. Super-linear brain-size cost (brain_energy_size_exponent). size_cost = base × n_weights^exponent. Default 1.0 (linear, legacy); 1.5 gives Kleiber-style scaling. default-. s-brain-size ✅ via two routes: 0.4.2 base override (stable populations) 0.4.3 biological mechanisms (principled, population trade-).

title: clade 0.4.2 (2026-04-16)
text: Three small fixes flagged 0.4.1 kernel--biology docs: Graded predator-distance signal. _pred_dist now actually returns 1/(d+1) docstring claimed. New spec predator_sense_graded = TRUE (default); legacy binary available via = FALSE. Signal sensory inputs clamped [0, 1] signal channel matches convention sensory inputs. max_age cap deferred Gompertz senescence senescence_rate > 0, hard cap doesn’t mask age-dependent mortality. Legacy default (senescence_rate = 0) unchanged.

title: clade 0.4.2 (2026-04-16)
text: 1500-tick reruns s-baldwin s-plasticity 0.4.1 Tier 5A+5C mechanisms. s-plasticity: seasonal > stable direction confirmed. s-baldwin: canonical direction reversal pre-0.4.0 🔴 confirmed short timescales, disappears equilibrium — sigma also mediates behavioural variance, seasonal stress amplifies selection wide posteriors. Stays 🟠 (kernel-limited).

title: clade 0.3.0
text: vignettes/showcase.Rmd: Baldwin section gains honest “σ rises defaults; CMA-ES finds narrowing regime” caveat; Mimicry Niche sections document new 0.3.0 flags. vignettes/introduction.Rmd, vignettes/scenarios.Rmd: gallery tables surface batesian_mimicry shelter_occupancy_bonus. vignettes/s-mimicry.Rmd, vignettes/s-niche.Rmd: Key-parameters tables gain new flags; --found sections refreshed describe current (post-0.3.0) kernel rather pre-fix state. vignettes/s-parental-care.Rmd, s-parental-investment.Rmd: re-measured displayed specs fixed graduation pathway; --found quotes updated “n_juveniles = 0 throughout” actual current trajectories. vignettes/baldwin-effect.Rmd: Addendum section documents CMA-ES-discovered regime canalization emerges (grass_rate 0.027, learning_rate_init_mean 0.007). vignettes/figures/showcase_bnn_canalization_demo.png: previously orphaned (generator produced ); now emitted gen_fixed_patch_fig.R original filename canalization alias, keeping Baldwin articles sync live kernel biology.

title: clade 0.3.0
text: Batesian mimicry (specs$batesian_mimicry = TRUE). palatable mimic (toxicity = 0) whose signal matches toxic model species now benefits predator’s learned aversion — predator avoids mimic alongside true toxic prey. Repeated attacks palatable mimics decay predator’s aversion memory (Rescorla-Wagner toward 0), reproducing Bates–Wallace predator discrimination-learning cycle. Default FALSE — existing runs byte-identical. Reference: Bates (1862) Trans. Linn. Soc. 23:495–566; Ruxton, Sherratt & Speed (2004) Avoiding Attack. Heritable niche-construction benefit (specs$shelter_occupancy_bonus > 0). Agents occupying sheltered cell receive bonus × depth energy per tick, Odling-Smee, Laland & Feldman (2003) heritable-environment effect. Existing niche_construction semantics (grass suppression + persistence + decay) unchanged; new parameter additive defaults 0 (behavior change).

title: clade 0.3.0
text: R/run.R .validate_specs() now warns spatial_sorting = TRUE combined toroidal = TRUE. Shine et al. (2011) invasion-front dynamics require bounded grid; torus population centroid wraps “front” concept geometrically ill-posed. toxicity_cost_per_tick default raised 0.5 → 2.0. prior default equalled idle_cost, making toxicity effectively free (Zahavi 1975 handicap principle violated). 2.0 makes maximally-toxic agent pay ~4× idle baseline per tick. inst/julia/src/modules/disease.jl docstring now documents transmission density-dependent (per-neighbour contact, β · S · / N frequency-dependent SIR) disease_duration deterministic recovery period (delta-distribution, exponential). Includes mean-field R0 ≈ transmission_prob × 8 × disease_duration approximation.

title: clade 0.3.0
text: New dev/audit/calibration/ harness drives search_cmaes() scenario’s parameter subspace, per-scenario fitness function encoding biological claim. Parallel launcher (run_all.sh) runs 31 scenarios concurrently. Full Phase 7 results dev/audit/calibration/RESULTS.md. Headline discoveries: regime Baldwin effect emerge (grass_rate ≈ 0.027, learning_rate_init_mean ≈ 0.007) otherwise non-canalising foraging world; regime speciation fires (mutation_sd ≈ 1.31); cephalopod paradox (Liedtke & Fromhage 2019) reproduced short max_age high learning rate.

title: clade 0.3.0
text: BNN REINFORCE score function fixed. previous update rule mu[] += lr * advantage * sigma[], score function Gaussian policy. new rule mu[] += lr * advantage * (w_sample[] - mu[]) / sigma[]^2 matches exact score Williams (1992) Blundell et al. (2015) §3.2. new last_sample::Vector{Float32} field BNNBrain caches Thompson-sampled weights forward() use bnn_update!. likely main reason earlier runs showed Baldwin-effect sigma narrowing even favourable regimes. Parliament--genes penalty logic fixed modules/kin.jl. counter previously included cooperators across neighbours; now counts cooperative kin separately, penalty fires cooperative relatives outnumber non-cooperative relatives — consistent Haig (2000) intragenomic conflict suppression. Predator dedicated sensory architecture. seed_predators! now builds predator-specific brain architecture [15, hidden..., 5] brain’s n_inputs matches 15-element predator sense vector. Previously predators used prey’s architecture (dynamic size depending input_radius active sensory modules), producing silent dimension mismatch whenever defaults changed. Mutation-rate evolution uses per-agent trait. mutation_rate_evolution = TRUE, reproduce.jl now reads parent.mutation_sd base mutation rate meiosis. Previously global specs["mutation_sd"] used unconditionally, evolved trait never propagated. stress-hypermutation multiplier continues apply top. Parental-care graduation pathway wired . reproduce.jl:126-131 Phase 2 stub pushed offspring straight env.agents even parental_care = TRUE. Offspring now enter parent’s carried_offspring brood (care_load += 1), age via age_juveniles!, fed via feed_offspring!, graduate main agent pool via graduate_offspring! reach juvenile_independence_age juvenile_independence_energy. fix, n_juveniles always 0 s-parental-care s-parental-investment; , typical runs log hundreds carried juveniles. randperm now imported Clade.jl. Latent bug surfaced parental-care fix enabled graduate_offspring! code path: randperm referenced modules/parental_care.jl:153 imported using Random line. Include-order fix (inst/julia/src/brains/ann.jl → bnn.jl). _quantize_brain_weights!(::BNNBrain, ...) method defined ann.jl bnn.jl loaded; Julia 1.12 aborts module load UndefVarError: BNNBrain. method now lives bnn.jl alongside type, matching method’s dispatch target. Manifest regenerated Julia ≥ 1.11 compatibility (previously pinned Statistics 1.10 doesn’t ship 1.12). Project.toml unchanged.

Baldwin effect formalised Hinton & Nowlan (1987) requires stable global fitness peak; clade’s default world provide one. vignette’s 45-run 3×3 factorial plus run-length experiments (500/1000/2000/5000 ticks) establish rigorously. stress_hypermutation scales specs["mutation_sd"] transiently reproduction rather mutating per-agent ag.mutation_sd field, logged mean_mutation_rate stays flat even mechanism fires. observable signal transient rise genetic_diversity resource crashes.

title: clade 0.1.1 (development)
text: plasticity_init_mean default changed 0.0 0.3. previous default sat trait floor; mutation pressure push upward, mean_plasticity always 0 practice.  signal_evolution_drift default changed FALSE TRUE. Without drift, signals remained exactly 0 forever, making mate-choice experiments uninformative.

title: clade 0.1.1 (development)
text: Lamarckian evolution (lamarckian = TRUE): rl_mode "none", within-lifetime RL-updated brain weights written back parent’s genome meiosis offspring inherit learned solution directly. Implemented inst/julia/src/modules/lamarckian.jl. Distinct epigenetics module (inherits methylation marks, weight values) Baldwin Effect (leaves genome unchanged). References: Baldwin (1896); Weismann (1892); Jablonka & Lamb (2005). Discrete / quantized ANN weights (ann_weight_values): set numeric vector (e.g. c(-1, 0, 1) ternary weights), every synaptic weight bias snapped nearest allowed value genome expression. Applies "ann" "bnn" brain types. Biologically motivated evidence biological synapses operate discrete strength states (Bhumbra & Bhatt 2020). Enables symbolic formula distillation evolved ANNs (original MATLAB alife2025usra codebase). ANN weight regularisation (ann_regularization): per-tick energy penalty brain weight complexity. Two modes: "weight_magnitude" (L1 penalty, drives weights toward zero) "weight_count" (L0-like penalty, fixed cost per active synapse). Scaled ann_regularization_lambda (default 0.001). mean weight magnitude now logged every run mean_ann_weight_magnitude. References: Laughlin et al. (1998); Attwell & Laughlin (2001). Native Julia test directory (inst/julia/test/): unit tests quantization, regularisation, Lamarckian logic can run directly Julia without R side. Run julia --project=inst/julia inst/julia/test/runtests.jl. Module triage script (inst/scripts/triage_modules.R): runs module isolation 300 ticks reports whether key biological signal detected ([OK]), absent ([FLAT]), crashing ([ERR]). Run Rscript inst/scripts/triage_modules.R. expect_evolution() test helper (tests/testthat/helper.R): asserts logged trait moves directionally simulation run. Also consolidates duplicated skip_no_julia() definition helper.R individual test files longer need re-define . Non-toroidal grid (toroidal = FALSE): Julia modules now use wrap_or_clamp() helper either wraps (toroidal) clamps grid boundary (linear). Required spatial sorting invasion-front experiments defined front. Carrion pathogen reservoir (carrion_transmission_prob): scavenging = TRUE disease = TRUE, agents die infected deposit flagged carcass; agent scavenges becomes infected probability carrion_transmission_prob (default 0.0 — backwards compatibility). batch_seeds(): convenience wrapper around batch_alife() takes single specs object vector seeds returns named list results. quick_specs() / full_specs(): preset specs fast exploratory runs (50 agents, 200 ticks, 20×20 grid) publication-quality runs (200 agents, 1000 ticks, 30×30 grid).

title: clade 0.1.1 (development)

title: clade 0.1.0
text: First public release.

title: clade 0.1.0
text: Agent-based evolutionary simulation running entirely Julia via JuliaConnectoR, eliminating per-tick R/Julia boundary overhead. Toroidal grid logistic grass regrowth seasonal amplitude modulation. Diploid haploid genome meiosis, Mendelian dominance models, configurable crossover mutation rates. Six brain types: Bayesian neural network (BNN, default), multilayer perceptron (ANN), continuous-time RNN (CTRNN), gene regulatory network (GRN), random null model, stubs transformer synthesis brains. run_alife() — single simulation run; returns named list compatible get_run_data() plot_run(). batch_alife() — run multiple replicates sequence. default_specs() — fully documented parameter list sensible defaults.

title: clade 0.1.0
text: module -op flag FALSE; overhead zero. Disease / SIR (disease = TRUE): stochastic transmission, energy costs, recovery, immunity. Kin selection (kin_selection = TRUE): pedigree-based relatedness; donors transfer energy -related Moore-neighbourhood agent relatedness threshold. Cooperation (cooperation = TRUE): reciprocal altruism mediated heritable cooperation-level trait. Scavenging (scavenging = TRUE): carrion deposited agent death; scavengers gain energy decaying carcasses. Niche construction (niche_construction = TRUE): agents build shelters slow grass regrowth reduce predator damage; shelters decay stochastically. Epigenetics (epigenetics = TRUE): heritable methylation marks canalize BNN weight uncertainty (sigma); transgenerational epigenetic inheritance (TEI) transmits marks offspring configurable probability. Within-lifetime RL (rl_mode = "actor_critic"): REINFORCE baseline updates output layer agent’s neural network tick, driven energy-gain reward. Social learning (social_learning = TRUE): prestige-biased copying — agents blend fraction highest-energy neighbour’s output-layer weights policy. Brain size evolution (brain_size_evolution = TRUE): heritable brain_size trait (Float32, reference 1.0) modelling parental provisioning hypothesis (van Schaik et al. 2023; Griesser et al. 2023; Song et al. 2025). Larger brains incur per-tick idle-cost surcharge (expensive brain hypothesis) proportional cognitive foraging bonus. bootstrapping problem — large-brained offspring pay metabolic cost birth foraging advantage emerges — means brain size evolves parental_care = TRUE buffers infancy energy deficit. third effect — sensing quality — scales grass perception inputs brain_size ^ brain_size_sensing_exponent, giving larger-brained agents directional navigation advantage. Logged mean_brain_size env$progress. New parameters: brain_size_evolution, brain_size_init_mean, brain_size_mutation_sd, brain_size_min, brain_size_max, brain_size_cost_scale, brain_size_sensing_exponent.

Returns archive parameter sets covering behavioural descriptor space. search_cmaes() — CMA-ES optimisation via GA package. search_gradient() — finite-difference gradient ascent log parameter scale; backend-agnostic (Zygote.jl dependency).
